Pak Study MCQs · pages 351-400

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Who was empowered to establish new Legislative Councils in other provinces under the Act 1861?

Correct Answer: Governor-General
Show explanation
Explanation

Under the Indian Councils Act of 1861, the Governor-General of India was empowered to:

  • Establish new Legislative Councils in other provinces (e.g., Bengal, NWFP, Punjab).
  • Nominate additional members to the Central Legislative Council.
2

Who occupied Gujarat during Humayun reign?

Correct Answer: Bahadur Shah
Show explanation
Explanation

Bahadur Shah of Gujarat occupied Gujarat during the reign of Humayun, leading to conflicts between them.

Humayun later defeated him and temporarily took control of Gujarat.

3

The word Hindu is derived from which Sanskrit word?

Correct Answer: Sindhu
Show explanation
Explanation

The word "Hindu" is derived from the Sanskrit word "Sindhu," which referred to the Indus River.

Persians pronounced "Sindhu" as "Hindu," and it later came to describe the people living beyond the Indus.

4

Who conquered Gandhara in 326-327 BCE?

Correct Answer: Alexander the Great
Show explanation
Explanation

Alexander the Great conquered Gandhara in 326–327 BCE during his campaign into the Indian subcontinent.

Gandhara was a key region due to its strategic location and cultural significance.

5

Quaid-e-Azam died after serving Pakistan for ______?

Correct Answer: 1 year 25 days
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan was created on 14 August 1947, and Quaid-e-Azam died on 11 September 1948.

This makes his duration of service 1 year and 25 days as Pakistan’s first Governor-General.

6

Who was behind the formation of the Pakistan Republican Party?

Correct Answer: Islander Mirza
Show explanation
Explanation

The Pakistan Republican Party was formed in 1955 by Iskander Mirza, the then Governor General.

It was created to counter the Muslim League and support the One Unit Scheme and centralized authority.

7

Pakistan is located in which continent?

Correct Answer: Asia
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan, officially the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, is a country in South Asia.

Pakistan is the world's fifth-most populous country.

Pakistan is the world's second-largest Muslim population.

Pakistan is the 33rd-largest country by area.

The capital of Pakistan is: Islamabad

the Currency of Pakistan is: the Pakistani rupee

8

Which one of the following is a Rabi Crop?

Correct Answer: Wheat
Show explanation
Explanation

Wheat is a Rabi crop.

Rabi crop is grown in autumn or early winter

They harvested in spring or early summer

They are grown in October or November. 

major rabi crop in India and Pakistan is:

  • wheat, 
  • barley,
  • mustard,
  • sesame,
  •  peas.

Rabi crops are known as winter crops.

9

Faiz Ahmad Faiz is buried in _____?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

Faiz Ahmad Faiz  was a Pakistani poet and author of Urdu and Punjabi literature

Faiz Ahmad Faiz  Born: February 13, 1911, Narowal

Faiz Ahmad Faiz Died: on November 20, 1984, in Lahore

Spouse: Alys Faiz (m. 1941–1984)

Awards: Lenin Peace Prize, Nishan-e-Imtiaz, Nigar Awards, Avicenna Prize

Faiz's resting place in Model Town Graveyard, Lahore, Pakistan

In March 1951, Faiz was jailed due to the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case
10

Who is the writer of “Udas Naslain”?

Correct Answer: Abdullah Hussain
Show explanation
Explanation

Udaas Naslain

Author: Abdullah Hussain

Genre: Novel

Publisher: Sang-e-Meel Publications

Publication date: First 1964; last 2015

11

Ganda Singh Wala Border was connected ____?

Correct Answer: Kasur and Ferozpur
Show explanation
Explanation

The Pakistani village of Ganda Singh Wala served as the main border crossing between India and Pakistan in the 1960s & 70s.

It was closed to trade & transit in 1986 and the main border crossing was shifted north to Wagah.

******

RY 15-01-2023

12

When Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time?

Correct Answer: 1994
Show explanation
Explanation

On 2 August 1948, Pakistan national team, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara, officially went on to play their first international game and tournament against Belgium winning the game 2–0 at the 1948 London Olympics.

Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

 Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

1971

1978

1982

1994.

***

Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.


Pakistan played its first International Hockey matches in 1948

In 1994 year, Pakistan was the world champion of Hockey

In 1994 en Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time

ND21-2-2023



13

Which is the highest peak of salt range?

Correct Answer: Soan Sakaser
Show explanation
Explanation

The highest peak of salt range is Soan Sakaser.

The Salt Range is a mountain range in the north of the Punjab province of Pakistan.

The range extends along the south of the Potohar Plateau and the north of the Jhelum River.

The salt range is situated between rivers Soan and River Jhelum

Sakesar (Khushab District) is the highest mountain in the salt region.
675 meters is the average height of the salt range.

The Upper Indus plain area is located in the South of the Salt Range and east of Sulemania.

******

14

When was the first Pakistani Postal Stamp issued?

Correct Answer: 9th July, 1948
Show explanation
Explanation

The First Potsal Stamp of Pakistan was issued on 9th July, 1948,

First Potsal Stamp of Pakistan was designed by Abdul Rehman Cughtai and approved by Quaid-i-Azam on the occasion of the first Independence Day

****

ND10-1-2023

15

Deltas plain of Pakistan starts from _____?

Correct Answer: Thatta
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indus River Delta, also known as the Sindhudurgarh, is located in the southern part of Pakistan and begins from the city of Thatta.

The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar Lake

The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

National River of Pakistan: Indus River

6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

Indus River Length: 3,180 km

Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 

The upper Indus Plain starts from Kalabagh.

16

Name the ruler who laid the foundation of Muslim Indo Empire and died while playing Polo?

Correct Answer: Qutb ud-Din Aibak
Show explanation
Explanation

Qutub-ud-din Aibak died in an accident while he was playing polo.

He fell from a horse and was severely injured.

He Died on 14 November 1210 (aged 60) in Lahore.

Burial in Anarkali Bazaar, Lahore.

He was born in 1150 in Turkestan.


 Additional information:

Qutub-ud-din Aibak established the first independent Turkish Kingdom in India.

The first Islamic state was founded in 1206 by Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

He became the first Sultan of Delhi from 1206 to 1210.

After Muhammad Ghori's assassination in 1206.

  • He established the Delhi Sultanate (1206–1526),
  • And started the Mamluk Dynasty or Salve Dynasty.
17

Badshahi Mosque was built in which year?

Correct Answer: 1673
Show explanation
Explanation
The Badshahi Mosque was built in 1673 in Lahore.
It was built by Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb.
It is 3rd the largest mosque
Construction of Badshahi Mosque completed in:1673
Additional information:
In 1947, Pakistan uplifted the Badshahi Mosque and citadel Lahore as symbols of the state.
Architect: Nawab Zain Yar Jang Bahadur
18

Quaid e Azam was elected as a member of Imperial Legislative Council from the Muslim Constituency of Bombay Presidency in ______?

Correct Answer: 1910
Show explanation
Explanation

Quaid-e-Azam was elected as a member of the Imperial Legislative Council from the Muslim Constituency of Bombay Presidency on 2nd December 1910 and resigned on 1st January 1913.

19

Who established the Muhammadan Educational Conference?

Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
Show explanation
Explanation

The All India Muhammadan Educational Conference was an organisation promoting modern, liberal education for the Muslim community in India.

Also founder of Ali Garh movement, two nation theory ,Murad Abad school 

Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was born in 1817 in Delhi


Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was an Islamic reformer and philosopher


 Mir Muttaql was the father of Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.


Aziz-un-Nisa was the mother of sir syed Ahmed Khan's

He is buried right along with the Sir Syed Masjid inside Aligarh University, Aligarh UP India.

What is the average height of salt range?

Correct Answer: 675 meters
Show explanation
Explanation
675 meters is the average height of the salt range

The highest peak of salt range is Soan Sakaser

The Salt Range is a mountain range in the north of the Punjab province of Pakistan

The range extends along the south of the Potohar Plateau and the north of the Jhelum River.

The salt range is situated between rivers Soan and River Jhelum

Sakesar (Khushab District) is the highest mountain in the salt region

The Upper Indus plain area is located in the South of the Salt Range and east of Sulemania

21

The area between Ravi and Beas is called ______?

Correct Answer: Bari Doab
Show explanation
Explanation

Bist Doab – Between Beas and Sutlej

Bari Doab – Between Beas and Ravi

Rachna Doab – Between Ravi and Chenab

Chaj Doab – Between Chenab and Jhelum

Sindh Sagar Doab – Between Indus (Sindh) and Jhelum

22

The boundary commission appointed at the time of Independence was headed by?

Correct Answer: Lord Radcliffe
Show explanation
Explanation

Cyril Radcliffe was the chairman of the Border Commission for India and Pakistan, announced by Lord Mountbatten in 1947

The Commission consisted of four members from the Indian National Congress

and four from the Muslim League

On 17 August 1947, the borderline that separated India from Pakistan, known as the Radcliffe Line was revealed. 

The Radcliffe line is spread through the Rann of Kutch in Gujarat to the international border in Jammu in Jammu & Kashmir,

dividing India and Pakistan into two different countries.

ND23-4-2023

23

Quaid-e-Azam personal letters are saved in _____?

Correct Answer: National Archives Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Quaid-e-Azam's personal letters are preserved in the National Archives Islamabad.

These archives contain important historical documents related to Pakistan's founding.

Including letters and papers of key figures like Quaid-e-Azam.

He became the first Sultan of Delhi from 1206 to 1210.

Which is the highest peak of the Koh-e-Sufaid Range?

Correct Answer: Sikeram
Show explanation
Explanation

Koh e Sufaid, is the mountain range located exactly on the Pak-Afghan Border At Parachinar, Kurram Agency, FATA, PAKISTAN

Sikeram, the highest peak in Koh-e-Safaid Ranges rises to 4,760 m.
26

The height of the Himalaya range increases as it moves from _____?

Correct Answer: South to North
Show explanation
Explanation

Himalaya range has many of Earth's highest peaks, including the highest, Mount Everest - 8848, at the border between Nepal and China.
The Himalayas are spread across five countries Bhutan, China, India, Nepal and Pakistan.
27

Ranikot Fort is located in which province?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

Ranikot Fort is a Talpur fort near Sann, Jamshoro District, Sindh.

Ranikot Fort is also known as The Great Wall of Sindh.

The length of Ranikot Fort is 32 km

Ranikot Fort is believed to be the world's largest fort,

Ranikot fort's ramparts have been compared to the Great Wall of China.

Ranikot fort constructed during the administration of Nawab Wali Muhammed Leghari, the Prime Minister of Sindh during the Talpur dynasty.

Correct Answer: All of these

Explanation

Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

  • 1971
  • 1978
  • 1982
  • 1994
29

In which direction of Pakistan Russia lies?

Correct Answer: North-East
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan is located near the North-East side of Russia.

The distance from Pakistan to Russia is 4,337 kilometers.

The Soviet Union–Pakistan relations  dated back to 1948


30

Karez is the irrigation system of which province of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Balochistan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Karez is the irrigation system of Balochistan.
  • This ancient method involves underground channels that transport water from aquifers to the surface.
  • It providing a sustainable water supply in arid regions.

Katpana is 2226 meters above altitude.

34

How Many Words in National Anthem of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 48 words
Show explanation
Explanation

About National Anthem

National Anthem was written by Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952 

the music was produced by Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949.

It was officially adopted as Pakistan's national anthem on 13 August 1954.

Ahmed Rushdi recorded the National Anthem of Pakistan in 1954.

After 68 years Pakistan's National Anthem recorded

15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan

48 Words in the National Anthem of Pakistan

The National Anthem of Pakistan was played for the first time in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran

In the National Anthem of Pakistan, " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for the Symbol of ambitions.

the first national anthem of Pakistan was played on Pakistan's national radio on 14 August 1947

80 sec is the duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan

which language is Pakistan's national Anthem written in? Persian

Which of the following mountain heights is not located in Karakoram?

Correct Answer: Tirich Mir
Show explanation
Explanation

Tirich Mir is located in the Hindu Kush range, not Karakoram.

Broad Peak and K2 are both part of the Karakoram range.

37

In Urdu, the name "Pakistan" literally means ______?

Correct Answer: Pure/Holy land
Show explanation
Explanation

  • The word Pakistan is related to the Persian and Urdu.
  • **
  • ***
  • The word Pakistan consists of two parts, i.e., Pak and Istan.
  • While Pak is a Persian word,
  • The word Pakistan means a holy/ pure/ clean place (country) and not the 'Land of the Pure'.
  •  The word Istan is from the word isthan, which is a Hindi word meaning a place.
  • ***

  • The word Pakistan was first introduced now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali. 

38

What is the maximum height of Kirthar Range?

Correct Answer: 2150 meters
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The elevation of the Kirthar Mountains is 2,260 meters (7,410 feet).
    • The highest peak in the Kirthar Range is Zardak Peak.
    • It stands at 7,430 feet (2,260 meters).
    • The Kirthar Range is situated along the border of Balochistan and Sindh provinces.
    • The hill station of Gorakh, located in the Kirthar Mountains, is situated off Dadu in Sindh.
    39

    The direction of Hindukush is from _____?

    Correct Answer: North-West
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Hindu Kush is a mountain range that runs from northeast to southwest, 

    Stretching from central and eastern Afghanistan into northwestern Pakistan

    The Hindu Kush range starts near the Pamir Mountains,

    It is an 800-kilometre-long (500 mi) mountain range in Central and South Asia to the west of the Himalayas.

    The highest peak of the Hindu Kush range in Pakistan is Tirich Mir

    Elevation:  (7,708) meters (25,289) 

    Additional information

    Pakistan Highest Peaks List

    Highest peak of Himalaya range is Nanga Parbat

    K-2 in Krakarm range

    Kirthar: Zardak peak

    Koh e Suleman :Takhat e Sulaimani

    Salt range : Sakesar

    Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

    Died: December 21, 1752, Bhit Shah

    Place of burial: Bhit Shah

    Nationality: Indian, Pakistani

    41

    Which is the youngest mountain range on the earth?

    Correct Answer: Himalayas
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Himalayas are the youngest mountain range on Earth.

    It is a new fold mountain formed by the collision of two (Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates) tectonic plates.

    They began rising around 40 to 50 million years ago.

    Which city in Punjab, Pakistan, is renowned for beautiful camel skin art, handicrafts, and glazed pottery?

    Correct Answer: Multan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Artisans in Multan are famous for creating camel-skin lamps with intricate naqashi painting, a tradition nearly 900 years old.

    Multan is considered one of the oldest cities in Pakistan.

    It is known for its rich history and Sufi heritage.

    It is located in the Punjab province of Pakistan.

    44

    Tipu Sultan had diplomatic relations with which French ruler?

    Correct Answer: Nepolean
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Tipu Sultan needs to a massive diplomatic campaign with the French (Napoleon and King Louis XVI), and the Ottoman.

    In 1788 Tipu Sultan, the ruler of Mysore in South India sent a diplomatic mission to Versailles to meet with King Louis XVI.

    Tipu Sultan handed over the command to important military and diplomatic missions when he was just 17 years of age.

    Tipu Sultan, also known as the Tiger of Mysore

    Fateh Ali was the real name of Tipu Sultan or Tiger of Mysore

    Tipu Sultan was the ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore based in South India

    Tipu Sultan  was Born: on December 1, 1751, in Devanahalli, India

    Tipu Sultan Died: on May 4, 1799, in Srirangapatna, India

    45

    Zulifqar Ali Bhutto was hanged to death for the murder of _______?

    Correct Answer: Nawab Muhammad Ahmad Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was hanged for the murder of Nawab Mohammad Ahmad Khan, a political rival, during his time in power.

    Bhutto's hanging followed a controversial trial in 1979 under General Zia-ul-Haq's regime.

    46

    General Ghulam Muhammad was dethroned by ______?

    Correct Answer: Islander Mirza
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    General Ghulam Muhammad was dethroned by President Iskander Mirza in 1958.

    Iskander Mirza declared martial law and dissolved the national and provincial assemblies, taking control of the government.

    47

    Islamia College Peshawar was established by ______?

    Correct Answer: Abdul Qayyum
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Islamia College is a public university located in Peshawar.
    • Founded by Abdul Qayoom Khan in 1913.
    • Quaid-e-Azam visited 3 times to Islamia College Peshawar in:
    • 1936
    • 1945 
    • 1948
    48

    When did Lord Mountbatten arrive in Delhi as new Viceroy of India?

    Correct Answer: Mar 22, 1947
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In March 1947, Mountbatten was appointed Viceroy of India and oversaw the Partition of British India into India and Pakistan. 

    The legislature representatives of the Indian National Congress, the Muslim League, and the Sikh community came to an agreement with Lord Mountbatten through "The Indian Independence Act of 1947"

    This plan is known as the 3 June Plan or Mountbatten Plan.

    This plan was the last plan for independence.

    ND17-1-2023

    49

    At the time of partition only ______ industries came under the jurisdiction of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 34
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    At the time of partition in 1947, only 34 out of 921 industries came under the jurisdiction of Pakistan.

    These industries were part of the assets and resources inherited by Pakistan from the British Raj after independence.

    50

    According to Area which is the Largest District of KPK?

    Correct Answer: Chitral
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    KPK is a province of Pakistan.

    Located in the northwestern region

    KPK has 36 districts and 8th divisions.

    Khyber Pakhtunkhwa is the fourth-largest province of Pakistan by land area

    and the third-largest province by population. 

    The local languages of KPK are? Hindko, Pashto (major), Chirtali, Hindo (major)

    What is the most famous dish of KPK? Chapal kabab

    The Largest District of KPK by Area of Chitral is 57 km²

    Total Area: 39,282 mi²

    What is the total forest area of KPK? 1.21 million hectares

    Capital and largest city: Peshawar ( sixth-largest in Pakistan.)

    Established (as NWFP): 1 July 1970

    NWFP became KPK on 15 April 2010 after: 18th amendment

    The old name of KPK was NWFP

     NWFP become KPK on 15 April 2010

    The province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) after the FATA Merger consists of 35 districts and 7th divisions.
    ND13-5-2023
    51

    Entry of marriage was made compulsory in ______ by Ayub Khan’s regime in 1961.

    Correct Answer: Union council
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In 1961, under Ayub Khan’s regime, the Muslim Family Laws Ordinance was introduced.

    It made registration of marriages compulsory in the Union Council to maintain official records and prevent disputes.

    52

    Arid region consists of ______?

    Correct Answer: Thal and Cholistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Arid regions are dry areas with little rainfall.

    In Pakistan, Thal Desert and Cholistan Desert are examples of such arid regions.

    53

    An important river of the Pothohar Plateau is ______?

    Correct Answer: Swan river
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Soan River is an important stream of the Pothohar or North Punjab region of Pakistan.

    It drains much of the water of Pothohar.

    Salt Range, series of hills and low mountains between the valleys of the Indus and Jhelum rivers.

    54

    Rabi crops (such as wheat, barley, gram) are harvested in ______?

    Correct Answer: Spring
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Rabi crops (e.g., wheat, barley, gram) are sown in winter (October–December) and harvested in spring (March–April).

    This contrasts with Kharif crops (e.g., rice, cotton), which are sown in summer and harvested in autumn.

    55

    Under Sub-Tropical Coastal region includes the areas of _______?

    Correct Answer: Balochistan and Sindh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan's Sub-Tropical Coastal Region primarily includes the coastal areas of:

    • Sindh (e.g., Karachi, Thatta, Badin)
    • Balochistan (e.g., Gwadar, Ormara, Pasni)

    These areas experience a hot, humid climate influenced by the Arabian Sea, with distinct monsoon patterns.

    56

    In Kashmir, Karewa deposits are the signs of ______?

    Correct Answer: Origin of a vast lake
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Karewa deposits in Kashmir are lacustrine (lake) deposits formed by the drying up of ancient lakes.

    These fertile sediments contain fossils and fine silt, ideal for agriculture like saffron cultivation.

    57

    Which city of Pakistan has Solar Power Station?

    Correct Answer: Quetta
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Quetta Solar Power Project is Pakistan's first grid-connected solar power station, established in Quetta, the capital of Balochistan.

    The project takes advantage of high solar irradiance in the region to produce clean, renewable energy.

    58

    Ajrak is the cultural dress of which province?

    Correct Answer: Sindh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ajrak is a traditional block-printed shawl and symbol of Sindhi culture.

    It is commonly worn during cultural events and gifted as a sign of respect in Sindh province.

    There are 22 processes in Ajrak printing and is very tedious and time consuming. 

    59

    Under which article of the constitution President can dissolved National Assembly?

    Correct Answer: 58 (2) b
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 29th May 1988, the Assembly was dissolved by the President by using the power acquired under Article 58(2)(b)

    while

    the Prime Minister of Pakistan to dissolve the National Assembly under Article 58 (1) read with Article 48(1) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

    60

    Which percentage of Pakistan's demand for crude oil is met by local production?

    Correct Answer: 10%
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan meets only about 10% of its crude oil demand through local production.

    The remaining 90% is imported to fulfill the country's energy needs.

    61

    Sialkot is famous for _______?

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Sialkot is a city located in Punjab, Pakistan.
    It is the 13th most populous city in Pakistan.
    It is located in the north-east Punjab.
    It is one of Pakistan's most industrialized regions.
    The postal code of Sialkot is  51310.
    The calling code of Sialkot is 052.
    Sialkot is known for manufacturing and export of many items:
    • Surgical instruments
    • Musical instruments
    • Sports goods
    • Leather goods
    • Textile goods
    62

    The high range of mountains of Pakistan in the north is known as _____?

    Correct Answer: Karakorum
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Karakorum Range is located in the north of Pakistan.

    It includes some of the world's highest peaks like K2.

    It lies between the Himalayas and the Hindu Kush ranges.

    63

    What is the minimum age to become a member of Provincial Assembly?

    Correct Answer: 25 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to Article 113 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the minimum age to become a member of the Provincial Assembly is 25 years.

    The candidate must also be a registered voter in any part of Pakistan.

    64

    Who first voiced the idea of a Muslim state in India?

    Correct Answer: Muhammad Iqbal
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Allama Iqbal first voiced the idea of a separate Muslim state during his presidential address at the All-India Muslim League session in Allahabad in 1930.

    He envisioned a separate homeland for Muslims in northwest India, which later inspired the creation of Pakistan.

    65

    Which province of Pakistan has no Desert?

    Correct Answer: KPK
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Khyber Pakhtunkhwa is a province in Pakistan with no desert.

    Total deserts in Pakistan:

    1. Thar Desert (in Sindh)
    2. Kharan Desert (in Balochistan)
    3. Katpana Desert in Skardu     (Gilgit Baltistan)
    4. Thal Desert in     Bhakkar (Punjab)
    5. Cholistan in     Bahawalpur (Punjab)


    Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026
    66

    Who is considered as the first poet of Pashto?

    Correct Answer: Ameer Kror
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ameer Khusro is considered the first poet of Pashto and Urdu

    The Full name of Amīr Khusrau : Ab'ul Hasan Yamīn ud-Dīn K͟husrau

    Khusrau is credited for the invention of the sitar.

    Amīr Khusrau lived under the Delhi Sultanate. 

    Amīr Khusrau  Born: 1253, Patiyali

    Amīr Khusrau  Died: on October 1325, in Delhi, India

    The Place of burial of Amīr Khusrau: Hazrat Nizamuddin Aulia Dargah, New Delhi, India

    while

    Baba Farid is the first Punjabi poet.

    Allama Iqbal is the National poet of Pakistan

    Hassan Bin Sabit was the first poet of Islam.


    67

    In 1965, the issue of Ran Kuch between Pakistan and India was resolved with the efforts of ______?

    Correct Answer: Britain
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 involved skirmishes between April and September 1965.

    Pakistan claimed the 24th Parallel as the boundary in the Rann of Kutch but India does not recognize it.

    The "Agreement of Rann of Kutch" was signed on 30 June 1965.

    It resolving the territorial dispute through British mediation.

    68

    What percentage of Pakistan’s total electricity generation is accomplished by thermal power plants?

    Correct Answer: 60
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Major Sources of Electricity in Pakistan

    Thermal: 60% share

    Hydro: 30% share

    Nuclear: 9% share

    Renewables: 1% share

    Major Sources of Electricity/Energy in the World

    Oil & Gas: 53% share

    Coal: 27% share

    Renewables: 13% share

    Nuclear: 7% share

    69

    First summit of SAARC was held in 1985 in?

    Correct Answer: Dhaka
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    First SAARC Summit, Dhaka, 1985

    Second SAARC Summit, Bangalore, 1986

    Third SAARC Summit, Kathmandu, 1987

    Fourth Summit, Islamabad, 1988

    Fifth SAARC Summit, Male, 1990

    Sixth SAARC Summit, Colombo, 1991

    Seventh SAARC Summit. Dhaka, 1993

    Eighth SAARC Summit, New Delhi 1995

    Ninth SAARC Summit, Male, 1997

    Tenth SAARC Summit, Colombo, 1998

    Eleventh SAARC Summit, Kathmandu, 2002

    Twelfth SAARC Summit, Islamabad, 2004

    Thirteenth SAARC Summit, Dhaka, 2005

    Fourteenth SAARC Summit, New Delhi, 2007

    Fifteenth SAARC Summit, Colombo, 2008

    Sixteenth SAARC Summit, Thimphu, 2010

    Seventeenth SAARC Summit, Addu City, 2011

    Eighteenth SAARC Summit, Kathmandu, 2014

    Nineteenth SAARC Summit, Islamabad, 2016 (Postponed)

    Twentieth SAARC Summit, Colombo, 2020

    ND13-1-2023

    70

    The city of ________ is famous for the craft of paintings on earthenware.

    Correct Answer: Gujarat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Gujarat (in Pakistan) is well known for its traditional ceramic art and paintings on earthenware, especially handcrafted pottery.

    This craft is part of the region’s cultural heritage, often featuring colorful floral and geometric patterns.

    71

    The Shimla Conference of 1945, failed due to _____?

    Correct Answer: Demand by Quaid-i-Azam to have all Muslims seats to be allocated to be the Muslim League
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Shimla Conference of 1945 failed primarily due to the demand by Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah that all seats allocated for Muslims in the central legislature should be controlled by the Muslim League.

    The Indian National Congress and Hindu leaders were unwilling to accept this, as they favored a broader representation for Muslims in the Indian political system.

    72

    During the period 1923-1934, Muslim League was divided in ___ factions.

    Correct Answer: Two
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    During the period 1923-1934, the All India Muslim League was divided into two factions: one led by Nawab Salimullah Khan and the other by Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

    This division occurred due to internal disagreements and differences regarding the direction of the Muslim League, but it was later unified under Jinnah's leadership.

    73

    Baltoro glacier in the Karakorum Range is about ______?

    Correct Answer: 62 KM long
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Baltoro Glacier is located in the Karakoram range of Pakistan.

    The Baltoro Glacier, at 62 km in length.

    It is located in the Shigar district Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan.

    74

    Who was the first Muslim ruler who tried to reconcile the differences between Muslims and Hindus?

    Correct Answer: Akbar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Mughal Emperor who promulgated a new faith entitled “Din-E-Elahi” in 1581 AD was Akbar

    ****

    Akbar was born: 1542

    Akbar died: 1605

    Mughal Emperor Akbar was born in:Sindh

    Akbar was the ruler Mughal emperor from 1556 to 1605.

    Mughal Emperor Akbar is buried: Agra

    Mughal Emperor Akbar was the third Emperor from Mughal Family.

    75

    Who wrote a book titled “Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind”?

    Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind  was written by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.

    Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind first published in the Urdu language in 1859.

    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan wrote the book Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind to analyze the Reasons for the Indian Revolt of 1857.

    76

    سبی اور جیکب آباد میں درجہ حرارت کہاں تک پہنچ جاتا ہے؟

    Correct Answer: پچاس سینٹی گریڈ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    سبی اور جیکب آباد پاکستان کے گرم ترین علاقے شمار ہوتے ہیں۔

    گرمیوں میں ان شہروں کا درجہ حرارت اکثر پچاس سینٹی گریڈ تک پہنچ جاتا ہے۔

    77

    Balochistan province is located in ______ of Pakistan.

    Correct Answer: South West
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Important Facts about Baluchistan:

    Location:

    Baluchistan is located in the South-West of Pakistan.

    It lies on the Iranian Plateau.

    Geographical Importance:

    It is the largest province by area, covering about 42% of Pakistan's total land.

    Baluchistan became a separate province in 1970.

    Administrative Details:

    Capital: Quetta

    Divisions: 7 total

    1. Kalat
    2. Makran
    3. Nasirabad
    4. Quetta
    5. Sibi
    6. Zhob
    7. Rakshan

    Districts: 35 total

    Geography & Rivers:

    There are many rivers, but 7 are considered main rivers.

    The Hingol River is the largest river in Baluchistan.

    Culture:

    The famous traditional dish Sajji belongs to Baluchistan.

      78

      علی گڑھ کالج میں _____ کی تعلیم سب کے لیے لازمی نہیں تھی۔

      Correct Answer: دینیات
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      علی گڑھ کالج (1875ء) میں سر سید احمد خان نے دینیات کو لازمی نصاب سے خارج رکھا تاکہ تمام مذاہب کے طلبہ کو یکساں تعلیمی مواقع مل سکیں۔

      لازمی مضامین: انگلش، فارسی، اور سائنس کو جدید ترقی کے لیے بنیادی سمجھا جاتا تھا۔

      79

      جنرل ضیاء الحق نے ______ 1977 کو ملک پر مارشل لاء لگایا۔

      Correct Answer: پانچ جولائی
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      جنرل ضیاء الحق نے 5 جولائی 1977 کو وزیراعظم ذوالفقار علی بھٹو کی حکومت ختم کرکے مارشل لاء لگا دیا۔

      انتخابات میں دھاندلی کے الزامات اور اپوزیشن کے احتجاج کے باعث فوج نے مداخلت کی۔

      80

      مصطفی کمال اتاترک نے کس چیز کے خاتمے کا اعلان کر دیا؟

      Correct Answer: خلافت
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      مصطفی کمال اتاترک نے 1924 میں خلافت کے خاتمے کا اعلان کیا۔

      ترکی میں سیکولر نظام متعارف کروا کر خلافت کو ختم کر دیا۔

      81

      پاکستان ایک ایسا ملک ہے جس کی تقریباً ______ فیصد لوگ گھریلو صنعتوں میں کام کرتے ہیں۔

      Correct Answer: 80
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      پاکستان میں گھریلو صنعتیں (کٹیج انڈسٹریز) صنعتی مزدوروں کا تقریباً 80 فیصد حصہ ملازمت فراہم کرتی ہیں۔

      تاہم، یہ اعداد و شمار مجموعی قومی افرادی قوت کا نہیں بلکہ صرف صنعتی مزدوروں کا حصہ ظاہر کرتے ہیں۔

      82

      After the defeat, Mughal King Bahadur Shah Zafar was exiled to ____?

      Correct Answer: Burma
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Mughal King Bahadur Shah Zafar was exiled to Rangoon, Burma after his defeat.
      • The British exiled him to Rangoon in 1858 after the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
      • He was the last Mughal emperor of India, reigning from 1837 to 1857, and was also a poet, musician and calligrapher.
      83

      خلافت عثمانیہ دنیا بھر کے مسلمانوں کا ______ مرکز تھا؟

      Correct Answer: روحانی
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      خلافت عثمانیہ دنیا بھر کے مسلمانوں کا روحانی مرکز تھا، کیونکہ اس کا دارالحکومت، استنبول، مسلمانوں کے لیے اہم مذہبی اور روحانی مقام تھا۔

      خلافت عثمانیہ نے مسلمانوں کی مذہبی قیادت فراہم کی اور امت مسلمہ کے اتحاد کی علامت تھی۔

      84

      بیسویں صدی کے اوائل میں _____ کی سربراہی مہاتما گاندھی کو دی گئی۔

      Correct Answer: کانگریس
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      بیسویں صدی کے اوائل میں مہاتما گاندھی کو  کانگریس کی سربراہی دی گئی۔

      گاندھی نے ہندوستان میں برطانوی راج کے خلاف غیر تشویش پسند تحریک کی قیادت کی اور کانگریس کے ذریعے ہندوستانی عوام کے حقوق کے لیے جدوجہد کی۔

      85

      مرزا غالب کس شہر میں پیدا ہوئے؟

      Correct Answer: آگرہ
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      مرزا اسداللہ خان غالب 27 دسمبر 1797 کو آگرہ میں پیدا ہوئے۔

      86

      In 2004, Musharraf got a vote of confidence from the National Assembly for remaining President for the next _____?

      Correct Answer: 5 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On January 1, 2004, Pervez Musharraf secured a vote of confidence from Pakistan's National Assembly and Senate.

      This allowed him to remain President until 2007 (a 5-year term under the 17th Constitutional Amendment).

      87

      In the presidential election of 1988, who was the supported by PPP and IJI?

      Correct Answer: Ghulam Ishaq Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In the 1988 presidential election, PPP and IJI supported Ghulam Ishaq Khan.

      He won the election after the assassination of General Zia-ul-Haq, defeating PPP candidate Nawabzada Nasrullah Khan.

      In 1993, when Ghulam Ishaq Khan was President, Nawaz Sharif was the Prime Minister of Pakistan.

      He served as 7th President of Pakistan

      In office: 17 August 1988 – 18 July 1993

      On 29th May 1988, General Zia-ul-Haq dismissed the government of Muhammad Khan Junejo to extend military rule.

      88

      Who won the Presidential election in 1993?

      Correct Answer: Farooq Ahmad Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Farooq Ahmad Khan Leghari won the Presidential election in 1993.

      He served as the 8th President of Pakistan from 1993 to 1997.

      He was elected after the resignation of Ghulam Ishaq Khan and the brief interim presidency of Wasim Sajjad.

      89

      In December 1981 Gen. Zia ul Haq announced the formation of Majlis-e-Shura, the Majlis-e-Shura was composed of _______?

      Correct Answer: 50 members
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In December 1981, General Zia-ul-Haq announced the formation of Majlis-e-Shura as a consultative body.

      It was composed of 50 nominated members from various sectors, including professionals, scholars, and technocrats.

      90

      M-2 Islamabad to Lahore motorway was inaugurated to which year?

      Correct Answer: 1997
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The M-2 Motorway or the Lahore–Islamabad Motorway.

      Length: 375 km

      Constructed: 1992

      North end: Kashmir Highway Interchange, Islamabad

      South end: Thokar Niaz Baig, Lahore

      ***

      The M8 Motorway is the longest one in Pakistan

      ****

      The Motorway M5 (Multan-Sukkur section)

       M-3 (Lahore-Abdul Hakeem)

      ND23-4-2023

      91

      کرنل محمد شیر خان کے مطابق سفارش سے نمٹنے کا واحد یہ ہے کہ ______؟

      Correct Answer: اس کو چور سمجھ کر خوب بے عزت کیا جائے
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      کرنل محمد شیر خان شہید (نشانِ حیدر) ایک اصول پسند اور دیانت دار فوجی افسر تھے، جو میرٹ اور انصاف پر یقین رکھتے تھے۔

      ان کے مطابق، سفارش کے کلچر کو ختم کرنے کے لیے سفارش کرنے والے کو چور سمجھ کر خوب بے عزت کرنا چاہیے تاکہ یہ رجحان ختم ہو۔

      92

      On April 23rd 1989, the PPP Government launched the People works program in _______?

      Correct Answer: NWFP
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The People's Works Program (PWP) was launched by the PPP government on April 23, 1989, in NWFP to address regional development and provide employment opportunities.

      It was launched by Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto, to improve infrastructure and promote socio-economic development in the province.

      93

      According to the new Local Govt. Ordinance 2005, Every Union Council will have ______?

      Correct Answer: 13 seats
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      According to the new Local Government Ordinance 2005, every Union Council was to have 13 seats.

      These seats included both general seats and reserved seats for women, peasants, workers, and minorities.

      94

      The new Local Government announced by the National Reconstruction Bureau consisted of ______?

      Correct Answer: Three tiers
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Local Government system introduced by Pakistan's National Reconstruction Bureau in 2001 established a three-tier structure:

      1. District Government (Top tier)
      2. Tehsil/Town Government (Middle tier)
      3. Union Council (Grassroots tier)

      This reform was part of General Musharraf's devolution plan under the Local Government Ordinance 2001.

      95

      Name the Pakistani woman police officer who will receive Excellence in Performance Award 2025 from International Association of Women Police (IAWP)?

      Correct Answer: Ayesha Butt
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Punjab Police officer SP Aisha Butt is set to receive the Excellence in Performance Award 2025 from the IAWP.

      The award honours her "distinguished service and commitment to policing,".

      SP Butt will be presented the award during the IAWP's in September 2025.

      The Annual Training Conference of IAWP will be held in Glasgow, Scotland. 

       

      Additional Information:

      IAWP stands for International Association of Women Police.

       ***

      96

      Former prime minister Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto has posthumously been awarded with which award on occasion of Pakistan day 2025?

      Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Pakistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      President Asif Ali Zardari posthumously awarded former prime minister Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto with Nishan-e-Pakistan in recognition of his services in the field of public service.

      Awarded on 23 March 2025

      His daughter Sanam Bhutto received the award.

       

      Additional Information:

      Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was a Pakistani politician & founder of Pakistan People's Party (PPP).

      He became ninth prime minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977.

      He was executed on 4 April 1946.

      ****

      97

      What is the title of official anthem of Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2025?

      Correct Answer: X Dekho
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      About Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2025

      Edition: 10th

      Participating teams: 6

      Starting date: 11 April 2025

      Closing date: 18 May 2025

      Administrator: Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB)

      Total Matches: 34

      Official Anthem: X Dekho

      ****

      98

      Lord Curzon divided Bengal into how many provinces?

      Correct Answer: Two
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lord Curzon divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905.

      99

      Who was the last ruler of the Mughal Dynasty?

      Correct Answer: Behadur Shah Zafar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Behadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal Emperor who was imprisoned by the English in Myanmar.

      Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal emperor of India (reigned 1837–57).

      He was a poet, musician, and calligrapher, more an aesthete than a political leader.

      Bahadur Shah Zafar was born October 24, 1775, in Delhi, India

      Bahadur Shah Zafar died November 7, 1862, in Rangoon [now Yangon], Myanmar.

      100

      ________ was the founder and first Emperor of the great Mughal dynasty in India.

      Correct Answer: Babur
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Babur was the founder and first Emperor of the great Mughal dynasty in India.

      Babur came to India originally from Fergana.

      Born: February 14, 1483, Andijan, Uzbekistan

      Died: December 26, 1530, Agra, India

      101

      Mughal Empire in India lasted from?

      Correct Answer: 1526-1857
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mughal Empire lasted from 1526 to 1857.

      Babar invaded the kingdom of the Lodi Afghans in northern India in 1526.

      There are 17 Mughal families who rule in India.

      First Mughal Emperor: Babar

      Last Mughal Emperor: Behadur Shah Zafar.

      102

      Who was Cyril John Radcliffe?

      Correct Answer: Lawyer
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Cyril John Radcliffe, was a British lawyer.

      He was a Law Lord, best known for his significant role in the Partition of India.

      He served as the first chancellor of the University of Warwick from its foundation in 1965 to 1977. 
      Born: March 30, 1899, United Kingdom
      Died: April 1, 1977
      Nationality: British

      103

      Rachna doab is between _______?

      Correct Answer: Ravi and Chenab
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Five Important Doabs of Pakistan 

      Sindh Sagar Doab is b/w: Indus & Jhelum rivers 

      Jech Doab is b/w: Jhelum & Chenab rivers 

      Bari Doab is b/w: Ravi & Sutlej rivers

      Rachna Doab  is b/w: Ravi  & Chenab rivers  

      Bias Doab is b/w: Bias & Sutlej rivers 

      Doab Chaj is b/w: Chenab and Jhelum.



      104

      Which Mughal king made Lahore his capital for 14 years?

      Correct Answer: Akbar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Mughal emperor Akbar the Great made Lahore his capital for 14 years, from 1584 to 1598.

      During this time, Akbar also built the Lahore Fort and the city walls which had 13 gates.

      105

      What was Akbar's number in the Mughal dynasty?

      Correct Answer: 3rd
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Akbar was the third Mughal emperor after Babur and Humayun.

      He reigned from 1556 to 1605 AD.

      He was famously known as Akbar the Great.

      He was the son of Nasiruddin Humayun.

      He succeeded Humayun as emperor at the age of 13.

      106

      Gomal Zam Dam was inaugurated in 2013 at ______?

      Correct Answer: South Waziristan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Gomal Zam Dam is a gravity dam in South Waziristan Tribal District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

      The dam impounds the Gomal River.

      Gomal Zam Dam was inaugurated in 2013.

      107

      Prime Minister Imran inaugurated Peshawar Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) on ______?

      Correct Answer: August 13, 2020
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The BRT project started in October 2017.

      Built at a cost of Rs 70 billion.

      It features a 27.5 kilometre-long corridor track.

      Includes 31 stations along the main route.

      Has 7 feeder routes stretching 62 km.

      These feeder routes include 146 stops.

      The system is designed to facilitate thousands of passengers daily.

      108

      Which country's Navy hosted the Multinational Maritime Exercise AMAN-21?

      Correct Answer: Pakistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      ü  Pakistan has been hosting the maritime multinational naval drill ‘AMAN’ since 2007.

      ü   Participant: British, the U.S., Turkish, Chinese, Japanese, Russia, and other forces.

      ü  AMAN-21 is the 7th Multinational Maritime Exercise.

      109

      In 1954 which Saudi Arabian king visited Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Saudi Bin Abdul Aziz
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • King: Saud bin Abdulaziz
      • Year: 1954
      • Location: Pakistan (Islamabad)
      • Duration: Six-day state visit
      • Date: October 10th
      110

      What is the minimum age specified in the constitution of Pakistan to become President of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 45
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      45 is the minimum age specified in the constitution of Pakistan to become President of Pakistan in 1962.

      40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution.

      According to the constitution 1973, the minimum age for the President of Pakistan: are 45 Years.

      **

      • Minimum age for President of Pakistan: 45 Years
      • Minimum age for Governor of Pakistan: 35 Years
      • Minimum age for PM of Pakistan: 35 Years
      • Minimum age for Senator of Pakistan: 30 Years
      • Minimum age for National Assembly Member: 25 Years
      111

      سندھ پر حملہ کے وقت اسلامی حکومت کا مرکز تھا؟

      Correct Answer: دمشق
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      محمد بن قاسم کے سندھ پر حملے (712 عیسوی) کے وقت اسلامی حکومت بنو امیہ کے تحت تھی۔

      اس وقت دمشق خلافت بنو امیہ کا دارالحکومت تھا۔

      112

      How many Silver medals in Olympic won Pakistan Hockey team?

      Correct Answer: 3
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan's hockey team won three Olympic silver medals in 1956, 1964, and 1972.

      They also won three gold (1960, 1968, 1984) and two bronze (1976, 1992) medals.

      113

      The Simon Commission was welcomed with ______?

      Correct Answer: Black Flags
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Simon's commission was rejected by Indian National Congress and Muslim League.
      Simon Commission was appointed in 1928.
      It comprises 7 members of the British.
      It was opposed by both Jinnah & Gandhi.
      114

      According to 1973 constitution, who elects the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: National Assembly
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced the 1973 constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

      According to 1973 constitution, Provincial assembly, National assembly and senate elect the President.

      Whereas the prime minister is elect by only national assembly.

      Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.
      115

      The second busiest largest seaport of Pakistan is ______?

      Correct Answer: Bin Qasim
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Port Qasim (Bin Qasim) is Pakistan’s second-largest and busiest seaport after Karachi Port.

      It handles a large volume of cargo, including industrial and commercial shipments.

      116

      Who among the following was/were resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council against Rowlatt Act?

      Correct Answer: Mohammed Ali Jinnah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mohammed Ali Jinnah resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council in protest against the Rowlatt Act of 1919, which he deemed oppressive and unjust.

      It is also known as the Anarchical and Revolutionary Crimes Act of 1919.

      It was passed on March 18, 1919, in Delhi.

      In the same year, Quaid-e-Azam's daughter, Dina, was born.

      117

      Where Baluchistan plateau is located?

      Correct Answer: South west of Pakistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Baluchistan Plateau covers most of Balochistan province, extending into Iran and Afghanistan.

      It is a dry, rugged, and mountainous region with significant mineral resources.

      118

      Who was the first winner of the first Hockey World Cup?

      Correct Answer: Pakistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 2 August 1948, the Pakistan national team, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara, officially went on to play their first international game and tournament against Belgium winning the game 2–0 at the 1948 London Olympics.

       Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

      1971

      1978

      1982

      1994.

      ***

      Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

      Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.

      119

      Pakistan Boy Scouts Association was founded in ______?

      Correct Answer: 1947
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Pakistan Boy Scouts Association was established in 1947 after the partition.

      It became a member of the World Organization of the Scout Movement in 1948.

      Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was the first Chief Scout of Pakistan Boy Scouts Association.

      120

      Pakistan won how many total medals in Special Olympics World Winter Games 2025?

      Correct Answer: 11 Medals
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      About Special Olympics World Winter Games 2025

      Edition: 12th

      Venue: Turin, Italy

      Events: 8 sports

      Starting: 8 March 2025

      Closing: 15 March 2025


      Additional Information:

      Pakistan finished the event with a total of 11 medals, including 6 gold, 3 silver, and 2 bronze.

       ***

      121

      کوسٹل ہائی وے کراچی کو ______ سے ملاتا ہے۔

      Correct Answer: گوادر
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      کوسٹل ہائی وے کراچی کو گوادر سے ملاتا ہے۔

      یہ شاہراہ مکران کوسٹ کے ساتھ ساتھ چلتی ہے اور بلوچستان کے ساحلی علاقوں کو جوڑتی ہے۔

      122

      مرزا غالب کے بزرگ ______ سے تعلق رکھتے تھے۔

      Correct Answer: افغانستان
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      مرزا غالب کے آباؤ اجداد افغانستان سے تعلق رکھتے تھے۔

      ان کے خاندان نے بعد میں ہندوستان میں سکونت اختیار کی۔

      123

      When was Quaid-e-Azam become the president of first constituent assembly?

      Correct Answer: 11 Aug 1947
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 11th August 1947, Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was elected as the President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.

      The first session of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held on 10th August 1947 at the Sindh Assembly Building in Karachi.

      Liaquat Ali Khan headed after the death of Jinnah for three years and produced the Objectives Resolution.

      ****

      124

      سطح مرتفع بلوچستان کوہ سلیمان اور کھیر تھر پہاڑی سلسلوں کے _____ میں واقع ہے۔

      Correct Answer: مغرب
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      سطح مرتفع بلوچستان کوہ سلیمان اور کھیر تھر پہاڑی سلسلوں کے مغرب میں واقع ہے۔

      یہ خطہ پاکستان کے جنوب مغربی حصے میں پھیلا ہوا ہے۔

      125

      پاکستان اور ازبکستان کے درمیان پہلا فضائی معاہدہ کہاں ہوا؟

      Correct Answer: اسلام آباد
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      پاکستان اور ازبکستان کے درمیان پہلا فضائی معاہدہ 1993 میں اسلام آباد میں ہوا۔

      اس معاہدے کا مقصد دونوں ممالک کے درمیان فضائی روابط کو فروغ دینا تھا۔

      126

      The _______ was a gathering of 35 prominent Indian Muslim leaders led by the Aga Khan III at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla in October 1906.

      Correct Answer: Simla Deputation
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In 1906, Sir Aga Khan met with a prominent Congress leader at the Viceregal Lodge in Shimla


      The Simla Deputation was a gathering of 35 prominent Indian Muslim leaders


      The       Aga Khan III led them at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla in October 1906.


      The deputation aimed to convince Lord Minto, then Viceroy of India, to grant Muslims greater representation in politics.


      127

      انگریز تاجروں کے تجارتی ادارے کا نام کیا تھا؟

      Correct Answer: ایسٹ انڈیا کمپنی
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      ایسٹ انڈیا کمپنی 1600 میں انگلینڈ میں قائم کی گئی تھی تاکہ ہندوستان اور ایشیا کے دیگر حصوں میں تجارت کی جا سکے۔

      یہ کمپنی بعد میں برصغیر میں برطانوی حکمرانی کی بنیاد بنی۔

      128

      A Hindu temple was attacked by the mob in district _____ in December 2020.

      Correct Answer: Karak
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In December 2020, a Hindu temple was attacked and set on fire by a mob in Karak district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

      The attack was condemned by Pakistani authorities, and the Supreme Court ordered its reconstruction.

      129

      کو ہندوستان میں مغربی تعلیم کا بانی تصور کیا جاتا ہے۔ ______

      Correct Answer: لارڈ میکالے
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      لارڈ میکالے کو ہندوستان میں مغربی تعلیم کا بانی تصور کیا جاتا ہے۔
      اس نے میکالے منٹس 1835 میں انگریزی تعلیم کو فروغ دینے کی حمایت کی، جس سے برطانوی تعلیمی نظام متعارف ہوا۔
      130

      مغربی پاکستان کے چاروں صوبوں اور ریاستوں کو یکجا کرکے 14 اکتوبر ______ کو ون یونٹ قرار دیا گیا۔

      Correct Answer: 1955
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      چودہ اکتوبر 1955 کو مغربی پاکستان کے تمام صوبوں اور ریاستوں کو ون یونٹ میں یکجا کیا گیا۔

      اس کا مقصد انتظامی سادگی اور مشرقی و مغربی پاکستان میں توازن قائم کرنا تھا۔

      131

      Due to the good treatment ______ of Mughals, the Rajputs had become loyal, but abusing these favors, the Rajputs got stronger.

      Correct Answer: Akbar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Emperor Akbar adopted a policy of religious tolerance and granted high ranks to Rajputs in his administration.

      This loyalty helped stabilize the Mughal Empire, but later, some Rajputs asserted independence.

      132

      Who got the vote of confidence from the National Assembly on May 27, 1993?

      Correct Answer: Nawaz Sharif
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On May 27, 1993, Nawaz Sharif received a vote of confidence from the National Assembly during his first tenure as Prime Minister (1990–1993).

      This occurred amid political tensions with President Ghulam Ishaq Khan, which eventually led to both leaders resigning in July 1993 as part of an agreement mediated by the military.

      Nawaz Sharif became the 12th Prime Minister of Pakistan on November 6, 1990, succeeding Benazir Bhutto.

      133

      When the Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan (IDBP) was established?

      Correct Answer: 1961
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan (IDBP) was established in 1961 to promote industrial growth.

      It provided financial assistance to small and medium-sized industries in Pakistan.

      Industrial Development Bank of Pakistan headquarters: Karachi

      134

      After independence, in 1947, the Pakhtoonistan stunt was raised in Khyber P.K. by?

      Correct Answer: Khan Abdul
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       After independence, in 1947, the Pakhtoonistan stunt was raised in Khyber P.K. by Khan Abdul

      135

      Rock salt range of the ________ famous all over the world.

      Correct Answer: Punjab
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Salt Range is located in Punjab and contains famous salt mines like Khewra, Kalabagh, and Warcha.

      It also has notable lakes such as Namal Lake, Khabikki Lake, and Uchhali Lake.

      Sakesar (Khushab District) is the highest peak in the Salt Range.

      Tilla Jogian (Jhelum District) is the second-highest point in the region.

      136

      ہمارا قومی ترانہ کونسی ہیت میں لکھی گئی ہے؟

      Correct Answer: مخمس
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      مخمس وہ شعری ہیت ہے جس میں ہر بند میں پانچ مصرعے ہوتے ہیں۔
      پاکستان کا قومی ترانہ حفیظ جالندھری نے مخمس ہیت میں لکھا تھا۔
      پورا قومی ترانہ بجنے میں ایک منٹ بیس سیکنڈ لگتے ہیں۔
      پاک قومی ترانے میں کل 15 مصرعے ہیں۔
      137

      Shah Jahan was the king of how many provinces?

      Correct Answer: 22
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      During Shah Jahan's reign, the Mughal Empire was divided into 22 provinces for administrative control.

      These provinces were established to improve governance and tax collection across the empire.

      Shah Jahan's real name was Shahab-ud-Din Muhammad Khurram.

      He built the Taj Mahal in memory of his wife, Mumtaz Mahal.

      138

      درج ذیل میں سے شہید ملت کس شخصیت کو کہا جاتا ہے؟

      Correct Answer: لیاقت علی خان
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      لیاقت علی خان پاکستان کے پہلے وزیرِاعظم تھے، جنہیں 16 اکتوبر 1951 کو راولپنڈی میں شہید کر دیا گیا۔

      ان کی شہادت کے باعث انہیں "شہیدِ ملت" کا لقب دیا گیا۔

      139

      At the time of the independence of Pakistan, who was the Chief of Pakistan Air Force?

      Correct Answer: Air Marshall Asghar Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      At the time of Pakistan's independence, the Chief of the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) was Air Vice Marshal (later Air Marshal) M. Asghar Khan. 

      From: 23 July 1957- 22 July 1965
      140

      After independence who was the Chief of Pakistan’s Naval Forces?

      Correct Answer: Rear Admiral Jefford
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Rear Admiral James Wilfred Jefford was appointed the first Commander of the Royal Pakistan Navy.

      He served as the Commander-in-Chief of the Royal Pakistan Navy until 1953. 

      He was a Royal Navy officer. 

      141

      The All India Muslim League’s meeting was held in Delhi on 20th March 1927, under the leadership of Quaid-e-Azam. He presented a set of proposals called ______?

      Correct Answer: Delhi Proposals
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Delhi Proposals 1927 (Key Points)

      Reasons:

      • Congress promoted Hindu interests and ignored Muslim demands.
      • Hindu Mahasabha (1923) threatened Muslim rights.
      • Need to protect Muslim political interests and discuss separate electorates.

      Demands:

      • 1/3 seats for Muslims in the Central Legislature.
      • Sindh to be separated from Bombay and given provincial status.
      • British reforms to be extended to Baluchistan & NWFP.
      • Muslim representation in Punjab & Bengal to be based on population.
      • Jinnah agreed to drop demand for separate electorates if these demands were met.
      142

      From where the skill of handicraft is inherited to Pakistani people?

      Correct Answer: From Ancient Times
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Handicraft means دستکاری

      The skill of handicrafts in Pakistan dates back to ancient civilizations like the Indus Valley Civilization (2500 BCE), where artisans crafted pottery, textiles, and jewelry.

      Traditional crafts such as embroidery, pottery, and woodwork have been passed down through generations, preserving the cultural heritage.

      143

      Civil Disobedience and non-cooperation movement were carried out jointly under the supervision of ______ and ______ in 1920.

      Correct Answer: Gandhi and Muhammad Ali Jauhar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Non-Cooperation Movement started in 1920.

      The leader of the Non-cooperation Movement was Mahatma Gandhia and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

      The non-cooperation movement was a mass movement that involved participation from the nationalists as well as the public. 

      After a series of events including the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in 1920.

      The Non-cooperation Movement was called off by Mahatma Gandhi after the Chauri Chaura incident in February 1922.

      144

      In which year Maharaja Ranjit Singh was crowned?

      Correct Answer: 1801
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Maharaja Ranjit Singh was crowned as the ruler of the Sikh Empire in 1801 at the age of 20.
      He established a strong and secular rule, uniting various Sikh misls under one empire.
      145

      Zulifqar Ali Bhutto appointed Mumtaz Ali Bhutto as Governor of which province of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Sindh
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mumtaz Ali Bhutto was appointed as the Governor of Sindh by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto in 1971.

      He later served as the Chief Minister of Sindh in 1972.

      146

      The report which was presented by Nehru for the future Constitution of India was rejected by ______?

      Correct Answer: The Muslim League
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Nehru Report (1928) proposed a united India without separate electorates, which was unacceptable to the Muslim League.

    • Jinnah rejected it and later presented his Fourteen Points (1929) in response.
      • 147

      The Reshmi Romal Movement's headquarter was in ______?

      Correct Answer: Kabul
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Maulana Mahmood ul Hassan (1858-1920) was a Indian Muslim scholar and independence activist who founded the Tahrik-e-Reshmi Rumal (Silken Handkerchief Movement) in 1915.

      Its headquarters was in Kabul, where efforts were made to seek Afghan and Ottoman support against British rule in India.

      148

      In the mid nineteenth century, a famous political thinker of Afghanistan Syed Jamal-ud-Din started a movement of unity and harmony among the Muslims, known as ____?

      Correct Answer: Islamic Movement
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Syed Jamal-ud-Din Afghani started the Islamic Movement, also known as Pan-Islamism, in the mid-19th century.

      His goal was to unite Muslims globally against Western imperialism and promote Islamic revival.

      149

      Exhibition Ideas 2000 was organized in Karachi from November 14th to 17th 2000 for ______?

      Correct Answer: Economic
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Exhibition Ideas 2000 was organized in Karachi to promote economic activities, showcasing trade, industry, and investment opportunities.

      It aimed to boost business collaboration and highlight Pakistan's economic potential.

      150

      The tenure of Wafaqi Mohtasib is ______ years.

      Correct Answer: 4
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The tenure of Wafaqi Mohtasib is 4 years.

      Wafaqi Mohtasib is a government department formulated via presidential order I of 1983, by President General Zia ul Haq.

      151

      How much Afghans come to Pakistan after 1979 war?

      Correct Answer: 4.5 million
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      After the 1979 Soviet invasion, around 4.5 million Afghans took refuge in Pakistan, making it one of the largest refugee movements.

      The influx affected Pakistan’s economy, security, and demographics, with many refugees settling in camps and urban areas.

      152

      Diplomatic ties between Pakistan and _____ were established in 1952.

      Correct Answer: Japan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan and Japan established diplomatic ties in 1952, shortly after Japan’s post-war recovery.

      Both countries have maintained strong economic and trade relations, with Japan being a key investor in Pakistan.

      153

      At what age Ashoka became a king?

      Correct Answer: When he was only 20 years old.
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ashoka became the Mauryan emperor in 268 BCE at the age of 20 after the death of his father, Emperor Bindusara.

      He later became famous for spreading Buddhism and his Dhamma policy after the Kalinga War.

      154

      Which of the following is one of the largest donors of aid to Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Saudi Arabia
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Saudi Arabia has been one of the largest donors of financial aid and support to Pakistan, providing loans, grants, and oil assistance.

      Other major donors include China and international organizations like the IMF and World Bank, but Saudi Arabia has consistently provided substantial aid.

      155

      When Muhammad Bin Qasim attacked Sindh, the strength of the army at that time was ________ thousand soldiers.

      Correct Answer: 12000
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Muhammad ibn Qasim led the Muslim conquest of Sindh from Raja Dahir in the battle of Aror in 712 AD.


      156

      When did Mahmood Ghaznavi's successful attack on Somnath occur?

      Correct Answer: In 1025 A.D
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mahmood Ghaznavi successfully attacked the Somnath temple in 1025 A.D., looting its wealth and demolishing the structure.

      Mahmud of Ghazni: Turkish invader

      Mahmud of Ghazni attacked India: 17 times

      Mahmood Ghazni is known as the “Abductor of Scholars”.

      17th attack of Sultan Mehmood Ghazni on India was took place in 1027 AD.

      Somnath temple is located in Uttar-Pradesh of India.

      157

      When Pakistan Industrial Development Corporation (PIDC) was established?

      Correct Answer: 1952
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      PIDC is a federal government entity established in 1952.

      It was initially formed to develop the industrial base in Pakistan.

      PIDC operates under the Ministry of Industries and Production.

      It has played a key role in industrialization and economic growth.

      158

      The Karakorum High Mountain Peaks remain covered with snow throughout the year, in this range, there are at least ______ Glaciers.

      Correct Answer: 20
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Karakoram Range has at least 20 glaciers, making it one of the most glacier-rich regions outside the polar areas.

      Notable glaciers include Siachen, Baltoro, Batura, and Hispar.

      159

      Batura Glacier situated in ______ valley is about 57 Kilometers long.

      Correct Answer: Hunza
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Batura is the second largest Glacier of Pakistan.

      Batura length:  64.5 km (57 km)

      Batura is in Karakoram mountain ranges.

      Gojal is also called Upper Hunza.

      160

      The Britishers cruelly and inhumanly killed those who took part in the war (war of independence), 100 Indians were killed to avenge _____ British soldier/s.

      Correct Answer: Single
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      After the 1857 War of Independence, the British adopted a brutal policy of repression, executing and punishing countless Indians.

      In some instances, 100 Indians were killed in retaliation for the death of a single British soldier, highlighting the severity of British vengeance.

      161

      The famous Lake “Hamoon Mashkel” is present in Baluchistan Plateau. It is the lake of _____?

      Correct Answer: Salt water
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lake Hamoon Mashkel is a saltwater lake.

      It is located in the Washuk District of Balochistan, Pakistan.

      It is part of the Balochistan Plateau.

      It is known for its saline water.

      162

      In 1848, British entered Punjab and annexed Punjab into the British control after defeating the ______?

      Correct Answer: Sikhs
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      British defeated the Sikh Empire in the Second Anglo-Sikh War (1848–1849).

      This led to the annexation of Punjab into British India in 1849.


      163

      The first gas field Pakistan discovered in which year?

      Correct Answer: 1952
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Sui gas field is the biggest natural gas field in Pakistan.

      It is located near Sui in Balochistan.

      The gas field was discovered in late 1952 and the commercial exploitation of the field began in 1955.

      164

      Animal Husbandry University is located in which city?

      Correct Answer: Lahore
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The University of Veterinary and Animal Sciences (UVAS), formerly known as Lahore Veterinary College,

      It is a public research university located in Lahore

       Established in 1882

      165

      Largest stadium of athletics of Pakistan was present in?

      Correct Answer: Islamabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The largest stadium for athletics in Pakistan,

      With a capacity of 48,700

      ,t is the Jinnah Sports Stadium in Islamabad. 


      166

      The Peshwa dynasty of Marathas was founded by?

      Correct Answer: Balay Vishwanath
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Peshwa dynasty of Marathas was founded by Balaji Vishwanath,

      He was the father of Baji Rao I. 

      He is considered the founder because his son, Baji Rao I, inherited the position of Peshwa after his death and expanded the Maratha empire. 

      167

      Act which allowed the Indians to compete for civil service jobs was?

      Correct Answer: Charter Act of 1853
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Charter Act of 1853 allowed the entry of Indians into the civil services through a system of open competitive examination.
      168

      Which former Prime Minister of Pakistan has been awarded Nishan e Pakistan on occasion of Pakistan Day 2025?

      Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      President Asif Ali Zardari posthumously awarded former prime minister Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto with Nishan-e-Pakistan in recognition of his services in the field of public service.

      Awarded on 23 March 2025

      His daughter Sanam Bhutto received the award.

       

      Additional Information:

      Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was a Pakistani politician & founder of Pakistan People's Party (PPP).

      He became ninth prime minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977.

      He was executed on 4 April 1946.

      ****

      169

      Pakistan is in the _____ subcontinent.

      Correct Answer: North - west
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan is located in the north-western part of the South Asian subcontinent.

      It shares borders with India (east), Afghanistan and Iran (west), China (north), and the Arabian Sea (south).

      170

      Bahadur Shah Zafar was forced to live in semi-imprisonment in the gatehouse of the ______?

      Correct Answer: Red Fort
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal emperor, was exiled by the British after the 1857 War of Independence.

      He was kept in semi-imprisonment in the Red Fort (Delhi) before being sent to Rangoon (Burma), where he died in 1862.

      171

      Some ______ came to the subcontinent to raid for treasure and to make prisoners.

      Correct Answer: Invaders
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Invaders such as Mahmud of Ghazni, Muhammad Ghori, and the Mughals raided the Indian subcontinent for wealth, prisoners, and territorial expansion.

      Their invasions led to the establishment of various dynasties and the spread of new cultures, religions, and administrative systems in the region.

      172

      Caliph _____ demanded Raja Dahir to return his prisoners and treasure?

      Correct Answer: Walid
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Caliph Al-Walid I (Umayyad Caliph) ordered Muhammad bin Qasim to lead the expedition against Raja Dahir in 712 AD.

      Raja Dahir, the ruler of Sindh, refused to return Muslim prisoners and treasure, leading to the Umayyad conquest of Sindh.

      173

      Which Pakistani won gold medal in ITF world championship Mixed Doubles category held in March 2025?

      Correct Answer: Aisam-ul-Haq Qureshi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan’s tennis player Aisam-ul-Haq Qureshi and Aqeel Khan made history by winning gold in the men’s 45+ doubles category at the ITF Masters World Championship.


      Additional Information: 

      ITF Masters World Championship 2025 held in Manavgat, Turkey.

      ITF stands for International Tennis Federation.

      ****


      174

      Who among the following cricketers made fastest T20I Century for Pakistan in March 2025?

      Correct Answer: Hasan Nawaz
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Opening batter Hasan Nawaz scored the fastest T20I century for Pakistan during the third match against New Zealand at Eden Park in Auckland on 21 March 2025.

      Chasing a target of 205, Nawaz reached his maiden T20I century in just 44 balls, breaking the previous record of 49 balls held by Babar Azam, set against South Africa in 2021.

      ***

      175

      Who was a Muslim commander in the state of Mysore?

      Correct Answer: Haider Ali
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Haider Ali (1721–1782) was the ruler of Mysore and a powerful Muslim military commander.

      He expanded Mysore’s territory and resisted British colonial forces during his reign.

      176

      The _______ were peaceful farmers.

      Correct Answer: Indus valley
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Indus Valley Civilization (c. 3300–1300 BCE) was known for its peaceful and agrarian society.

      Its people engaged in farming and trade, with no significant evidence of warfare or militarization.

      177

      The houses at Mohenjo Daro were made of ______?

      Correct Answer: Mud bricks
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The houses at Mohenjo Daro were primarily built using sun-dried and baked mud bricks.

      This construction method was common in the Indus Valley Civilization due to the availability of clay and the region's hot climate.

      178

      Pakistan is the ______ largest producer of cotton in the world.

      Correct Answer: 5th
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan is the fifth-largest producer of cotton globally, following China, India, Brazil, and the United States. 

      In the 2024/2025 period, Pakistan's cotton production is projected at 5 million 480-pound bales, accounting for approximately 4% of global production. 

      179

      Potwar Plateau is situated in between ______ hills in north and plain area of Punjab in south.

      Correct Answer: Kala Chitta Range
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

      The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

      Located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River
      ******

      • The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the current four districts:

        Jhelum

        Chakwal

        Rawalpindi

        Attock

        ****

        The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

        Potohar Plateau سطح مرتفع پوٹھوہار .consists of:

        • District Rawalpindi District
        • Chakwal District
        • Jhelum District
        • Khushab District,
        • Mirpur District
        • Islamabad
        • Mianwali District,
        • Sargodha District (Northern Belt)
      180

      Khwaja Farid Uddin was the grandfather of _______?

      Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Khwaja Farid Uddin was the grandfather of Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.

      He was a prominent reformer and founder of Aligarh Movement.

      Sir Syed Ahmed Khan played a key role in modern education for Muslims in British India.

      181

      Lord Wavell convened a conference of Political Parties in June 1945 in _______?

      Correct Answer: Simla
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lord Wavell convened the Simla Conference in June 1945 to discuss India's political future.

      The main issue was the representation of Muslims, but the talks failed due to disagreements between the Muslim League and Congress.

      182

      Malwa was annexed to ______ by Allauddin Khilji in 1305.

      Correct Answer: Delhi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Alauddin Khilji annexed Malwa to the Delhi Sultanate in 1305 after defeating the Paramara dynasty.

      His general, Ain-ul-Mulk Multani, led the conquest and became the governor of Malwa.

      183

      Juna Khan was the son of _______?

      Correct Answer: Ghiyasuddin Tughluq
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Juna Khan, later known as Muhammad bin Tughluq.

      He was the son of Ghiyasuddin Tughluq, the founder of the Tughluq Dynasty in the Delhi Sultanate.

      He became the Sultan of Delhi in 1325 after his father’s death.

      184

      ______ Cloth Industry is one of the famous cloth industries of the world.

      Correct Answer: Faisalabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Faisalabad is known as the "Manchester of Pakistan" due to its large textile industry.

      It is a major hub for cloth manufacturing, textile mills, and exports.

      185

      Ghiyas Uddin ruled for only ______ years.

      Correct Answer: Five
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ghiyas-ud-Din Tughlaq, he ruled from 1320 to 1325, which is five years.

      Ghiyas-ud-Din Balban ruled the Delhi Sultanate from 1266 to 1287, a total of 21 years.

      186
