      In the history of Indo-Pak, Aryan period is that when Aryans were living in the _______ of it.

      Correct Answer: North West
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Aryans migrated and settled in the North-Western regions of the Indian subcontinent around 1500 BCE.

      This area includes present-day Pakistan and northwestern India, where the early Vedic civilization developed.

      187

      Ayub Khan removed/deposed which politician in 1958?

      Correct Answer: Iskander Mirza
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 7 October 1958 Martial law was declared by Ayub Khan.

      He removed Iskander Mirza and took over the office as president of Pakistan.


      Additional information

      History:

      President Ayub Khan resigned from office on March 25, 1969.

      Ayub Khan was the commander-in-chief of the Pakistan Army.

      And became the president of Pakistan after President Iskandar Ali Mirza in 1958.

      He was the second president of Pakistan, serving from 1958 to 1969.

      He was succeeded by General Yahya Khan.

      188

      ______ play an important part in a country’s economy.

      Correct Answer: Exports
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Exports generate revenue for a country by selling goods and services to other nations.

      They contribute to economic growth, trade balance, and foreign exchange reserves.

      189

      Sub-Himalayas are also known as ______?

      Correct Answer: Siwalik
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sub-Himalayas are also known as Siwalik or Outer Himalayas.

      Sub-Himalayan range of the northern Indian subcontinent.

      Their average height varies between 600 and 1200 meters,

       The range spans the modern-day countries of :

      1. Pakistan,
      2. India,
      3. Nepal,
      4. and Bhutan.


      *********


      190

      Which university bestowed honorary doctorate degree on Rahat Fateh Ali Khan?

      Correct Answer: Oxford
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Rahat Fateh Ali Khan received an honorary doctorate from Oxford University in 2019.
      This was in recognition of his contributions to music and culture.
      191

      The word Pakistan was firstly introduced in __________ in 1933.

      Correct Answer: Now or Never
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The word Pakistan was firstly introduced in now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.

      Now or never was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: Punjab, Afghanistan, Kashmir, Sindh and Baluchistan. (Pakistan)

      192

      The important port of Arabian Sea is ______?

      Correct Answer: Karachi
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Karachi is Pakistan’s largest and busiest seaport on the Arabian Sea.
      It handles the majority of the country's international trade.
      193

      Which one was the first province in Pakistan which imposed lockdown to stop the spread of Covid-19 on March 23, 2020?

      Correct Answer: Sindh
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Sindh was the first province in Pakistan to impose a lockdown on March 23, 2020, to control the spread of Covid-19.
      The decision was taken by the Sindh government, led by Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah.
      194

      The doctrine of Wahdat ash-shuhod' was propounded by Shaykh Sirhindi to counter 'wahdat al-wujod'(unity of being) which had been advanced by?

      Correct Answer: Muhiyiddinibn Arabi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ibn-al-Arabi propounded the Sufi philosophy of wahdat-ul-wujud 

      Wahdat-ul-Wujud means onceness of being.

      He was one of the greatest Muslim philosophers. 

      195

      The famous Babar.Nama (or Tuzk-e-Babari) authored by Emperor Babar was written in which language?

      Correct Answer: Chagatai
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Tuzk-e-Babri, was written initially by Emperor Babur in Chagatai Turkic, which he referred to as "Turki"

      Tuzuk-i-Baburi is written by Babur.

      Babarnama is also known as Tuzuk-i-Baburi.

      Babur and Jahangir are the only two emperors of Mughal Empire who wrote their own biographies.

      196

      Ahmad Shah founded the very first unified state in Afghanistan in?

      Correct Answer: 1747 AD
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ahmad Shah Durrani founded the first unified state in Afghanistan, the Durrani Empire, in 1747. 

       Ahmad Shah Abdali was Born in 1722, in Afghanistan.

      He died in October 16, 1773, in Afghanistan

      197

      In which year was the educational institute of Delhi established in Indo-Pak?

      Correct Answer: 1825
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Delhi College, a significant educational institute in the Indo-Pak region, was established in 1825. 
      It was closed down in 1877 and reopened in 1948. 
      Other important 19th-century educational institutions in the region include:
      Dar-ul-Uloom Deoband (established in 1866) 
      Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College (established in 1875, which later became a university in 1920). 
      The University of Delhi, a major institution of higher learning, was founded in 1922. 
      198

      The argo based industry is ?

      Correct Answer: dairy farm
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Dairy farming is a class of agriculture for the long-term production of milk

      199

      2000 years old statue was found at ancient site of Mount Dillu Roy in Kot Chutta, ______ district.

      Correct Answer: Dear Ghazi Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Mount Dillu Roy is an ancient archaeological site located in Dera Ghazi Khan district, Punjab, Pakistan.

    • It contains historical artifacts, including a 2000-year-old statue from the Buddhist period.
      • 200

      How many times Martial Law has been imposed in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 4
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2025:

      Sr#

       Martial Law Number

      Date of Martial Law

      Martial Law Implemented by

      1

      First Martial Law

      7th October 1958

      Iskandar Mirza

      2

      2nd Martial Law

      25th March 1969

      General Yahya Khan

      3

      3rd Martial Law

      5th July 1977

      General Zia ul Haq

      4

      4th Martial Law

      12th October 1999

      General Pervaiz Musharraf


       

      There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2025.

      201

      Humayun was defeated by Sher Khan in the battle of Chaunsa, during that time Humayun was compelled to take refuge in ______?

      Correct Answer: Iran
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • After his defeat in the Battle of Chaunsa (1539) by Sher Shah Suri, Humayun lost control of his empire.

    • He took refuge in Iran, where Shah Tahmasp provided him with military support to regain his throne.
      • 202

      Sultan Mehmood Ghaznavi became a King in the age of ______?

      Correct Answer: 27 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni, founder of the Turkic Ghaznavid dynasty.

      He was ruled from 997 to 1030 AD.

      He became king at 27.

      He led multiple invasions into India.

      He was born on November 2, 971 AD, in Ghazni, Afghanistan.

      He was died on April 30, 1030 AD.

      203

      Ashoka ruled India for ______?

      Correct Answer: 40 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Emperor Ashoka ruled India from 268 BCE to 232 BCE, which is a period of 40 years.

    • He was a Mauryan emperor known for spreading Buddhism and promoting peace.
      • 204

      The old name of Taxila was _____?

      Correct Answer: Takhshasila
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       The old name of Taxila was Takhshasila.

      Taxila was designated a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1980.

      Taxila's Province in Punjab.

      Taxila's Division is Rawalpindi Division.

      Taxila's District is Rawalpindi District.

      Taxila's Tehsil is Taxila Tehsil.

      Old Taxila was the capital of Ghandhara.

      Old Taxila was an important city in ancient India.

      Taxila is one of Punjab's popular tourist spots.

      205

      The European nations including Holland, ______ and England came to India as traders.

      Correct Answer: France
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The European nations that came to India as traders included Holland, France, and England.

    • The French East India Company was established in 1664 to trade in India.
      • 206

      The Nehru Report's recommendations were presented in all parties conference in 1928 at ______?

      Correct Answer: Lucknow
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Nehru Report of 10 August 1928 was a memorandum to appeal for a new dominion status and a federal set-up of government for the constitution of India.
      Quaid e Azam presents his 14 points in the response of Nehru report on 9 March 1929.
      These points covered all of the interests of the Muslims.
      It was prepared by a committee of the All Parties Conference chaired by Motilal Nehru with his son Jawaharlal Nehru acting as a secretary.
      There were nine other members in this committee.
      207

      Who was Bande Ali in relation to Muhammad Ali Jinnah?

      Correct Answer: His brother
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Bande Ali Jinnah was one of Muhammad Ali Jinnah’s younger brothers.

      Muhammad Ali Jinnah had:

      Brothers:

      1. Bande Ali Jinnah
      2. Ahmad Ali Jinnah
      3. Rahmat Ali Jinnah

      Sisters:

      1. Fatima Jinnah
      2. Maryam Jinnah
      3. Shireen Jinnah
      208

      When was CPEC agreement signed between China and Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 20 April 2015
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is a series of energy and infrastructure projects, some of which connect China’s Xinjiang to Gwadar.

      Initiated in mid-2013 and formally launched in April 2015.

      CPEC is generally described as a $46 billion package of Chinese aid.

      209

      The minister for Indian affairs approved the ______ on June, 1905.

      Correct Answer: Partition of Bengal
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Partition of Bengal was approved by the British Indian government in June 1905.
      It was officially implemented on 16 October 1905.
      The partition was carried out by Lord Curzon.
      Bengal was divided into:
      1. East Bengal and Assam (Muslim-majority)
      2. West Bengal (Hindu-majority).
      Due to widespread opposition, the partition was annulled in 1911.
      210

      Who was the last Hindu king whose kingdom was spread over a vast in northern India?

      Correct Answer: Raja Harash
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Harsha (606–647 AD) was the last major Hindu king who ruled a vast empire in northern India.

    • His empire extended from Punjab to Bengal, but after his death, it fragmented due to invasions.
      • 211

      The Mughal Empire founded by Babur in 1526, came to an end in ______?

      Correct Answer: 1857
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mughal Empire lasted from 1526 to 1857.

      Babar invaded the kingdom of the Lodi Afghans in northern India in 1526 

      There are 17 Mughal  families who rule in India

      First Mughal Emperor: Babar

      Last Mughal Emperor: Behadur Shah Zafar

      212

      Before the 14th amendment, who was the Chief Justice of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Syed Sajjad Ali Shah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Syed Sajjad Ali Shah remained CJP: 1994 to 1997.

      Syed Sajjad Ali Shah was died in: 2017.

       

      213

      Muhamamd Rafiq Tarar was chosen as President of Pakistan by _______?

      Correct Answer: Muslim League
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Nawaz Sharif was PM in 1998.

      Pervaiz Musharraf was chief executive in 1998

      Rafiaq Tarar remained President: 1998 to 2001.

      Rafiaq Tarar died: March 2022.

      214

      What percentage of the country's crude oil needs does Pakistan import?

      Correct Answer: 90%
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Pakistan imports around 90% of its crude oil to meet domestic energy demands due to limited local production.

    • The country's reliance on imports increases its vulnerability to global oil price fluctuations.
      • 215

      Which was an important turning point in the freedom movement of Muslims of Indo-Pak?

      Correct Answer: Cabinet Mission
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Cabinet mission arrived India: March 1946 

      Cabinet mission sent by: British government


      216

      Which battle of Mysore is also known as Tipu Sultan`s battle against the British?

      Correct Answer: Third
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      A total of four Mysore Wars was fought between the British and the rulers of Mysore

      01.   1767 to 1769

      02.   1780 to 1784

      03.   1790 to 1792

      04.   1799

      while

      Tipu Sultan, also known as the Tiger of Mysore

      Fateh Ali was the real name of Tipu Sultan or Tiger of Mysore

      Tipu Sultan was the ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore based in South India

      Tipu Sultan  was Born: on December 1, 1751, in Devanahalli, India

      Tipu Sultan Died: on May 4, 1799, in Srirangapatna, India

      Tipu Sultan wrote a letter to France to help him against British Army rule in India. 

      Tipu Sultan was Buried: on May 5, 1799, Mausoleum of Tipu Sultan, Srirangapatna

      The third battle of Mysore is also known as Tipu Sultan`s battle against the British

      ND5.3.2023

      217

      Mirza Galib was died in which city?

      Correct Answer: Delhi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mirza Ghalib, one of the greatest Urdu and Persian poets.

      He was born in Agra on 27 December 1797.

      He passed away on 15 February 1869 in Delhi.

      218

      Prime MInister Nawaz Sharif participated in The 21st Session of the Conference of the Parties ( to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change ) held in which country?

      Correct Answer: Paris
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif is due to arrive in Paris to represent Pakistan at the 21st UN Climate Change Conference (COP21)

      They are being held in the French capital from November 30 to December 11

      To reach a new global deal to tackle climate change by cutting greenhouse emissions.


      219

      Which famous police officer has written the book 'Police, Crime and Politics Memories of an IGP'?

      Correct Answer: Hafiz S.D. Jammy
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Title Police, Crime & Politics: Memoirs of an IGP

      Author Hafiz S. D. Jamy

      Publisher Vanguard, 1997

      Original from the University of Michigan

      Digitized Oct 9, 2009

      220

      In 1911 on the occasion of his coronation at the Dehli Darbar King George V announced the?

      Correct Answer: Annulment of Partition of Bengal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The partition of Bengal was implemented in 1905, but it was not annulled during the Delhi Darbar.

      In 1911, Delhi Durbar was held in India to celebrate the occasion when King George V was crowned in England.

      The Delhi Durbar 1911 lasted for nine days.

      This Durbar was historic for two reasons:

      • one, it as the only one attended by the Emperor himself, and
      • second, this was where the shifting of the Imperial Capital from Calcutta to Delhi was announced.
      221

      In 1932 The British Prime Minister announced the Communal Award granting separate electorate to the depressed classes. Name the British Prime Minister.

      Correct Answer: Ramsay Macdonald
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      British Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald created the Communal Award on 16 August 1932.

      Also known as the MacDonald Award,

      It was announced after the Round Table Conference (1930–1932) 

      The background to the Poona Pact was the Communal Award of August 1932,

      They provided a separate electorate for depressed classes.

      In 1932, B.R. Ambedkar negotiated the Poona Pact with Mahatma Gandhi.


      222

      The Congress and the Muslim League boycotted the Simon Commission Who led the breakaway section of the Muslim League which supported the Simon Commission?

      Correct Answer: Mian Muhammad Shafi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      There became two groups in the All India Muslim League.

      One Group was called the Quaid Group and the second was the Shafi Group.

      223

      Chauburji in Lahore was the gateway to the garden built for Princess ____ daughter of Emperor Aurangzeb?

      Correct Answer: Zeb-un-Nisa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Chauburji is a Mughal era monument in the city of Lahore

      Often attributed to Mughal Princess Zeb-un-Nisa, daughter of Emperor Aurangzeb. 

      This Mughal-era monument, meaning "four minarets,"

      It is located in Lahore on Multan Road and was originally the gateway to a large garden. 



      224

      Which of the following Un Peace keeping Operations has been mandated to monitor the LOG (former Cease Fire Line) in Kashmir?

      Correct Answer: UNMOGIP
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The UN peacekeeping operation to monitor the LoC  is the UNMOGIP

      They supervise the ceasefire between India and Pakistan in the State of Jammu and Kashmir. 


      United Nations Military Observer Group in India and Pakistan stands for UNMOGIP

      It was established in 1949

      Despite the agreement on the "Line of Control" in 1972,



      225

      By the end of august 1947 the Hindu majority states had acceded to India except?

      Correct Answer: Both of them
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      By the end of August 1947, most Hindu-majority princely states in British India had joined India

      Except for a few, including Junagadh, Hyderabad, and Jammu and Kashmir

      At the time of independence, there were 565 princely states, which were not directly ruled by the British



      226

      First amendment in 1973 constitution of Pakistan was made to ______?

      Correct Answer: Redefines boundaries
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The first amendment to the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan, enacted on May 4, 1974
      Redefined Boundaries:
      The amendment addressed Pakistan's international and provisional boundaries, reflecting East Pakistan's loss. 


      227

      Why did the British annex Punjab?

      Correct Answer: To control trade routes
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The British annexed Punjab in 1849 after the Second Anglo-Sikh War to secure trade routes to Central Asia.

    • Punjab's strategic location made it important for British trade and military movements.
      • 228

      What was the impact of colonialism on Punjab?

      Correct Answer: Suppression of local craft and traditions
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      British colonial rule led to the decline of traditional crafts in Punjab as machine-made British goods replaced local handicrafts.
      Heavy taxation and economic policies favored British industries, causing economic hardship for local artisans.
      229

      Under CRPC, which section deals with peace on conviction?

      Correct Answer: Section 106
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Section 106 of CRPC provides for security for keeping the peace upon conviction.
      It applies when a person is convicted of offenses involving breach of peace.
      230

      Pakistan uses which platform for international remittances?

      Correct Answer: Western Union
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Western Union is a widely used platform for sending and receiving remittances in Pakistan.
      It allows fast international money transfers through banks and agent locations.
      231

      Which country is biggest Arms Provider of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: China
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      China is the biggest arms provider to Pakistan.

      It supplying 81% of Pakistan's arms imports between 2020 and 2024, according to a SIPRI report. 

      232

      Which is the most fertile region of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Indus Basin
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Indus Basin is the most fertile region due to its rich alluvial soil.

    • It supports major agricultural activities in Pakistan.
      • 233

      Which fort in Pakistan is listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?

      Correct Answer: Rohtas Fort
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The ancient fort accepted as a UNESCO World Heritage Site is Rohtas Fort, located in Punjab, added in 1997.

      It was built in the 16th century.

      It is renowned for its military architecture.

      It was constructed over seven years.

      234

      What is the required number of years of High Court practice to become a Supreme Court judge in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 5 years as a High Court Judge or 15 years as an advocate
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • A person must have 5 years of experience as a High Court Judge or 15 years as an advocate to qualify as a Supreme Court Judge.

    • This is stated in Article 177 of the Constitution of Pakistan.
      • 235

      Where is the M-3 Special Economic Zone under CPEC located?

      Correct Answer: Faisalabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The M-3 Special Economic Zone (often associated with the Allama Iqbal Industrial City) 
      • Location: Faisalabad, Punjab (near Sahianwala Interchange)
      • Total Area: Approximately 3,217 acres 

      CPEC Special Economic Zones (SEZs) – Total: 9

      1. Rashakai Economic Zone, M-1 (Nowshera)
      2. China Special Economic Zone (Dhabeji)
      3. Bostan Industrial Zone
      4. Allama Iqbal Industrial City (M-3, Faisalabad)
      5. ICT Model Industrial Zone (Islamabad)
      6. Industrial Park on Pakistan Steel Mills Land (Port Qasim, Karachi)
      7. Special Economic Zone (Mirpur, AJK)
      8. Mohmand Marble City
      9. Moqpondass SEZ (Gilgit-Baltistan)

      Last updated question by NS on 11 May 2026

      236

      A judge of the Supreme Court of Pakistan shall hold office until he or she attains the age of ______?

      Correct Answer: 65 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      A Judge of the Supreme Court can hold his office until the age of 65 years.
      While a High Court Judge can hold his office until 62 years.
      This age was decided in the 6th amendment of the constitution.
      Supreme Court comprises 17 Judges.
      237

      In which desert is the Derawar fort is located?

      Correct Answer: Cholistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The height of walls of Derawar Fort is 40 meters.

      It is located in Cholistan Desert, Ahmadpur East Tehsil, Bahawalpur.

      The walls have a perimeter of 1500 metres and stand up to 30 metres high.

      238

      When did the first economic war start between India and Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 1947
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The first major economic conflict between India and Pakistan occurred after independence in 1947.
    • It stemmed from the partition of British India and financial disputes.

    • Tensions escalated due to the Jammu and Kashmir issue.
    • This leading to the first Indo-Pakistani War (1947-1948).
      • 239

      Which city contributes the most to Punjab's GDP?

      Correct Answer: Lahore
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lahore is the largest city in Punjab.

      It serves as the province's economic, cultural, and industrial hub.

      It contributes significantly to Punjab's GDP through industries, services, trade, and commerce.

      240

      Under which section of the CrPC is an FIR lodged in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Section 154
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      FIR stands for First Information Report.

      It a document prepared by police under Section 154 of the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) 1898.

      It starting criminal proceedings in cognizable cases.

      241

      In which year was Rawal Dam constructed?

      Correct Answer: 1962
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Rawal Dam was built in 1962 by the Punjab Government.
    • It supplies water to Rawalpindi and Islamabad.
    • The dam is constructed on the Korang River.
    • The Korang River originates in the Murree Hills.
    • It flows toward Islamabad.
      • 242

      Out of 565 princely states in India, how many states acceded to Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 13
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      At the time of Indian Independence in 1947, there were 565 princely states.

      Out of these, 13 princely states decided to join Pakistan.

      Pakistan (including both West and East) constituted about 20% of the total area of British India.

      243

      Winter monsoon come from which side?

      Correct Answer: North East
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The winter monsoon in South Asia typically comes from the northeast, bringing dry, cool air from the land towards the ocean.

      This pattern is opposite to the summer monsoon, which brings rain.

      244

      Who is the head of the Punjab Bureau of Administration?

      Correct Answer: Chief Secretary Punjab
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Chief Secretary Punjab is the highest-ranking civil servant in the province, overseeing administrative affairs.
      They coordinate between departments and report directly to the Chief Minister of Punjab.
      245

      How many members of the National Assembly of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 336
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The National Assembly of the parliament consists of 336 members old was 342 reducing KPK from 61 to 55.

      A member of the National Assembly is called MNA

      National Assembly members are increased on the basis of Population.

      25 age is specified for members of the National Assembly to contest the election

      National Assembly is the lower  house of parliament 

      Senate is the Upper house of parliament 

      NamePunjabSindhKPKBaluchistanICTTotal
      General1416145163266
      Women3214104-60
      Non-Muslims-----10
      Total1737555203336

      According to Article 51 (1) and 3(a) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan

      Article 51. (1) There shall be [three hundred and thirty-six] seats for members in the National Assembly, including seats reserved for women and non-Muslims.

      246

      عبدالحسین شادانی کا اصل نام کیا تھا؟

      Correct Answer: وجاہت حسین
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       عندلیب شادانی کا اصل نام سید وجاہت حسین تھا ۔ان کی پیدائش اتر پردیش کے سرائے سنبھل میں 1895 میں ہوئی تھی

      247

      بانگ درا کس کی تصنیف ہے؟

      Correct Answer: علامہ اقبال
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      بانگ درا برصغیر کے عظیم شاعر و فلسفی محمد اقبال کی شاعری پر مشتمل پہلا مجموعہ کلام ہے جو 1924ء میں شائع ہوئی۔ اس میں اقبال کی مشہور نظمیں شکوہ، جواب شکوہ، خضر راہ اور طلوع اسلام شامل ہیں

      248

      Which agreement led to the formal end of the 1971 War between India and Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Simla Agreement
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Simla Agreement was signed on July 2, 1972, between India and Pakistan, marking the formal end of the 1971 war.

      It aimed to establish lasting peace and mutual respect for each country's sovereignty.

      249

      When Faiz Ahmed Faiz was died?

      Correct Answer: 20th November 1984
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Faiz was the first Asian poet to be awarded the Lenin Peace Prize (1962) by the Soviet Union 

      was also nominated for the Nobel Prize in literature.

      Faiz Ahmad Faiz is also the author of Urdu and Punjabi literature.

      Born: February 13, 1911, Kala Qader, in the Sialkot

      Died: November 20, 1984, in Lahore

      while 

      Name only two Pakistani who got Lenin Prize.

      Faiz Ahmad Faiz revived Lenin Prize in 1962.

       Abdul Satar Edhi revived Lenin Prize in 1988.

      ND28-4-2023

      250

      Is May 29 observed as Youm-e-Takbeer in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: False
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Youm-e-Takbeer is observed on May 28, marking Pakistan’s nuclear tests in 1998.

    • May 29 is not officially recognized as Youm-e-Takbeer.
      • 251

      Who is the Pakistan's 2nd largest trading partner?

      Correct Answer: European Union
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Pakistan's second-largest trading partner, after China, is the European Union (EU).
      252

      The first war fought between India and Pakistan after partition was on which issue?

      Correct Answer: Kashmir
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Jammu and Kashmir regions have been disputed since partition, with Pakistan and India both claiming ownership.

      October 1947 – January 1949: The first Indo-Pakistani war began following an invasion of Kashmir by armed tribesmen from Pakistan.

      253

      Who was the first Inspector General of Punjab after the creation of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Khan Qurban Ali Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Khan Qurban Ali Khan, was the first Inspector General of Punjab after the creation of Pakistan in 1947.

      The first Inspector General of Prisons for Punjab after the creation of Pakistan was Dr. C. Hathaway.

      254

      When did Pakistan first take a loan from the IMF?

      Correct Answer: 1958
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • First IMF Loan: December 8, 1958.
    • Loan Amount: US$25,000 (Equivalent to $272,463 in 2024).
    • Government: General Ayub Khan's era.

    • Purpose: Economic stabilization and fiscal management
      • 255

      Kohlu district is located in which province?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

    • It is known for its mountainous terrain.

    • It is home to the Marri tribe.
    • It has historical significance in Balochistan's politics.

      Which party won the 1977 elections in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

    • The opposition alleged rigging, leading to protests and eventually martial law by General Zia-ul-Haq.

      Correct Answer: Shaheen-III

      • Type: Two-stage, solid-fueled medium-range ballistic missile
      • Range: 2,750 kilometers (1,700 miles)
      • Test: First tested in 2015
      • Capabilities: Capable of carrying both nuclear and conventional payloads

      259

      Auditor General of Pakistan is appointed by _____?

      Correct Answer: President
      Show explanation

    • The Auditor General of Pakistan is appointed by the President under Article 168 of the Constitution.

    • The AGP serves a four-year term.
    • He is responsible for auditing government accounts.
      • 260

      The main tribes in North Waziristan are:

      Correct Answer: Both
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       The main tribes in North Waziristan are the Darwish, or Wazir, and the Dawar. 


      The tribes of Waziristan posed a major challenge to British supremacy along the north-west frontier from 1848 to 1947. 


      261

      Sir Agha Khan is buried in which city?

      Correct Answer: Aswan, Egypt
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Aga Khan III was one of the founders and the first president of the All-India Muslim League.

      He Died on July 11, 1957, in Versoix, Switzerland

      Place of burial: Aga Khan Aswan, Egypt.

      In 1906 he headed the Muslim deputation to the British viceroy

      He was Born on November 2, 1877, in Karachi



      262

      Majority Pakistan Population is associated with:

      Correct Answer: Agriculture
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Agriculture is considered the backbone of Pakistan's economy

      Pakistan's principal natural resources are arable land and water.

      Agriculture accounts for about 18.9% of Pakistan's GDP and employs about 42.3% of the labour force.


      263

      Quaid e Azam frequently visited which place during National movement:

      Correct Answer: Islamia College Peshawar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      He visited in 1936, 1945, and 1948. 


      264

      What was the operation launched after the Malakand incident?

      Correct Answer: Operation Rah e Rast
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Operation Rah-e-Rast was a military operation launched by Pakistan in 2007–2009

      Operation Rah-e-Rast was a military operation in the Swat Valley,

      Major General Nasser Janjua led Operation Rah-e-Haq in 2007,

      He successfully recaptured Swat by December 2007.

      In 2009, the military launched Operation Black Thunderstorm to retake Buner.

      265

      In which year did Gilgit-Baltistan become a semi-province?

      Correct Answer: 2009
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Gilgit-Baltistan became a semi-province in the year 2009 with the introduction of the Gilgit-Baltistan Empowerment and Self-Governance Order, 2009.

      This order granted the region:

      • A chief minister and a governor.
      • A legislative assembly with limited autonomy.
      266

      What was Pakistan's first investment plan?

      Correct Answer: 1955-1960
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan's first five-year plan, which ran from 1955 to 1960,

      They focused on industrialization, agriculture, and infrastructure development. 

      267

      The CPEC railway line near Havelian is called?

      Correct Answer: Taxila Khunjerb
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       The railway line near Havelian that is part of the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is called the Khunjerab Railway, or ML-5.

      The line runs from Taxila Junction to Havelian station

      The line is also known as the China–Pakistan railway. 

      The Taxila–Khunjerab Line is a feasibility study that was awarded in 2007. 

      268

      Lahore Resolution changed 'states' into 'state' by amendment in which year?

      Which Article of the 1973 Constitution provides that no personal shall be deprived of life or liberty save in accordance with law

      Correct Answer: Article 9
      Show explanation

      This article is part of the Chapter on Fundamental Rights.

      وفات: 10 دسمبر 1874ء

      میر انیس کی وجہ ٔ شہرت مرثیہ نگاری ہے جس میں آج تک اُن کے مقامِ سخن کو اُردو کا کوئی شاعر نہیں پہنچ سکا۔

      Which is the coldest place of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Skardu
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The coldest place in Pakistan is Skardu


      Skardu is a city in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan and serves as the capital of the Skardu District. 


      The coldest place in Pakistan can be the glacial parts of Gilgit Baltistan, where in winter the average temperature remains below -20.

      281

      The Simla delegation called on Lord Minto, the Viceroy of India in October 1906. The delegation was led by?

      Correct Answer: Sir Agha Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sir Agha Khan led the Simla Deputation

      ****

      Simla Deputation organized by the Indian Muslim leaders met the Governor-General and Viceroy Lord Minto in Simla on 1 October 1906. 

      The delegation of Simla deputation met Lord Minto on 1st October 1906

      Simla's deputation met with لارڈ منٹو

      شملہ وفد 1906

      1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

      2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

      3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

      4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

      5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

      6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.

      Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation

      Ayoub Khan enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962

      Yahiya Khan abrogated the 1962 constitution and became CMLA

      • Islamabad: 25

      Which of these is the largest forest of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Changa Manga
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Changa Manga Forest is the largest forest in Pakistan.
      It covering an area of approximately 12,510 acres (20.5 square miles).
      It is located in the Kasur district of Punjab, Pakistan.
      Changa Manga is a man-made (artificial) forest, established in 1866.
      294

      The Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms were in ______?

      Correct Answer: 1919
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Montagu-Chelmsford Report was published on July 8, 1918.
      • The report was the result of discussions between Edwin Montagu, secretary of state for India from 1917 to 1922, and Lord Chelmsford, the viceroy of India between 1916 and 1921.
      • The report was used as the basis for the Government of India Act of 1919.
      295

      What is the GDP growth of Pakistan in 2024?

      Correct Answer: 2.5%
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Economic Challenges: Pakistan's GDP growth for 2024 is around 2.5% due to inflation, fiscal deficits, and external debt pressures.

    • IMF & World Bank Projections: Both forecast 2-3% growth, reflecting economic constraints.
      • 296

      What is the name of 11th Army Chief of Staff in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Syed Asim Munir
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Here is the list of Pakistan's Army Chiefs:

      1. Tikka Khan (1972–1976) – 1st Army Chief.
      2. Zia-ul-Haq (1976–1988) – Longest-serving (12+ years).
      3. Mirza Aslam Beg (1988–1991) – Succeeded after Zia's death.
      4. Asif Nawaz Janjua (1991–1993) – Passed away in office.
      5. Abdul Waheed Kakar (1993–1996) – Oversaw Benazir's dismissal.
      6. Jehangir Karamat (1996–1998) – Resigned early.
      7. Pervez Musharraf (1998–2007) – Led 1999 coup, became President.
      8. Ashfaq Parvez Kayani (2007–2013) – First to get an extension.
      9. Raheel Sharif (2013–2016) – Led anti-terrorism operations.
      10. Qamar Javed Bajwa (2016–2022) – Served six years.
      11. Asim Munir (2022–Present) – Current Army Chief.

    • It was formed in 2020 to challenge the government.

    • It aimed to restore democracy, oppose military interference in politics, and protest alleged election rigging.

      Show explanation

      This induction was part of Pakistan's efforts to modernize its air force capabilities. 

      301

      _______ is a Pakistani jurist who has been Justice of the Islamabad High Court (IHC).

      Correct Answer: Lubna Saleem
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lubna Saleem Pervez and Sardar Muhammad Sarfraz Dogar are both Pakistani jurists who have served as Justices of the Islamabad High Court (IHC).

      Lubna Saleem Pervez

      • The first female judge of the IHC since it was established in 2011.
      • Born on February 14, 1965 
      • Graduated from the University of Karachi in 1991 with an LLB degree.
      • Worked as a lawyer in banking, corporate, commercial, and tax matters 

      Sardar Muhammad Sarfraz Dogar

      • Born on July 3, 1968
      • Served as a Justice of the Lahore High Court from 2015 to 2025
      • Appointed as Acting Chief Justice of the IHC in February 2025.

      Sami Ullah Khan is famous player of _____?

      Correct Answer: Hockey
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      He is a former field hockey player from Pakistan.

      He was nicknamed The Flying Horse because of his great speed.

      He is a former and veteran player of the Olympic Games.

      National Game of Pakistan: Field Hockey.

      When Pakistan first time take part in Olympics?

      Correct Answer: 1948
      Show explanation

      304

      Which top commander of Daesh has been captured in March 2025 with the help of Pakistani Security Forces?

      Correct Answer: Mohammad Sharifullah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mohammad Sharifullah, top commander of Daesh has been captured in March 2025 with the help of Pakistani Security Forces.

      On 5 March 2025, US President Donald Trump said the person responsible for killing 13 US service members during the withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021 had been arrested with the help of Pakistan.

      The 2021 Abbey Gate bombing killed 13 US service members and approximately 170 Afghan civilians during the chaotic US withdrawal from Afghanistan.

       The militant Islamic State (IS) group had claimed responsibility for the killings.

       ****

      305

      Name the IS terrorist and behind 2021 Kabul airport bombing arrested with the help of Pakistan in March 2025?

      Correct Answer: Mohammad Sharifullah
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On 5 March 2025, US President Donald Trump said the person responsible for killing 13 US service members during the withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021 had been arrested with the help of Pakistan.

      The 2021 Abbey Gate bombing killed 13 US service members and approximately 170 Afghan civilians during the chaotic US withdrawal from Afghanistan.

       The militant Islamic State (IS) group had claimed responsibility for the killings.

       ****

      306

      Who said that Muslims need a separate nation?

      Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Allama Iqbal proposed the idea of a separate Muslim nation in his 1930 Allahabad Address to protect Muslim identity and rights.

      He emphasized that Muslims and Hindus were distinct nations and that Muslims should have their own state.
      307

      What did Sir Syed Ahmed Khan say after observing the attitude of the Congress?

      Correct Answer: Not to join the Congress
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Sir Syed Ahmad Khan believed that Congress mainly represented Hindu interests and advised Muslims not to join it.
    • He encouraged Muslims to focus on education and avoid politics to secure their future.
      • 308

      Who Reviewed the famous book “The Indian Musalmans” in 1872?

      Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      'The Indian Musalmans' was written by W. W. Hunter in 1871. 

      Later it was reviewed by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan in 1872.

      309

      From where did the Europeans enter the Indian subcontinent?

      Correct Answer: Calicut
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama was the first European to enter the Indian subcontinent.
    • He landing at Calicut (Kerala) in 1498.
    • This marked the beginning of European colonial influence in India.
    • He leading to Portuguese, Dutch, British, and French settlements.
      • 310

      Debal is a famous _____?

      Correct Answer: Seaport
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Debal was a famous seaport located near present-day Karachi, Pakistan.
    • It is historically significant as the entry point of Muhammad bin Qasim during his 712 AD conquest of Sindh.
      • 311

      Which of the following Sufi saints was known as Garib Nawaz?

      Correct Answer: Khwaja Moinuddin Chisti
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti was known as "Garib Nawaz" (Benefactor of the Poor) due to his compassion and service to the needy.
    • He was a Sufi saint of the Chishti Order, who spread Islam in the Indian subcontinent, particularly in Ajmer, India.
      • 312

      Baba Fareed was born in _____?

      Correct Answer: Kothewal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Baba Farīd or Fariduddin Masud was born in 1188 (573 AH) in Kothewal

       He was a Sunni Muslim and was one of the founding fathers of the Chishti Sufi order.

      The Shrine of Baba Farid is a 13th-century Sufi shrine located in Pakpattan, Pakistan

      Baba Fareed Shaker Ganj' was the first poet of: Punjabi

      313

      The institution of Mohtasib was established by ______?

      Correct Answer: Presidential Order 1983
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The office of Wafaqi Mohtasib (Ombudsman) was established in 1983 by President Order I of 1983.

      The main functions entrusted to Wafaqi Mohtasib were to diagnose, investigate, redress and to rectify any injustice done to public through mal-administration of an agency of the Federal Government.

      314

      The ratio of Urdu Speaking population is ______?

      Correct Answer: 8%
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The percentage of Punjabi-speaking people in Pakistan is 39%.

      List of other Languages:

      Sindhi speaking in Pakistan: 15%

      Pashto speaking in Pakistan: 18%

      Urdu in speaking Pakistan: 8%

      Balochi speaking in Pakistan: 3%
      315

      The second major profession in Pakistan is _____?

      Correct Answer: Civil Service
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Civil Service is the second major profession in Pakistan after agriculture, employing a significant workforce in government administration.

      It includes bureaucracy, public administration, and policy-making.

      It playing a key role in governance and national development.

      316

      Gwadar remained under Oman's rule for ______ years.

      Correct Answer: 174
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in the Balochistan province of Pakistan

      The Gwadar Port was Opened: in 2002;

      Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from Oman on 8th September 1958.

      Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

      Gwadar remained under Omani rule for 174 years from 1783 -1958 

      Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in Balochistan province.

      Opened: 2002 

      while 

      Pakistan has three big sea ports  It was purchased from Oman in 1958.

      Karachi Port,

      Muhammad Bin Qasim port

      and Gwadar port.

      *******

      ND29-5-2023

      317

      The oldest Barrage on the Indus River is _______?

      Correct Answer: Sukkur Barrage
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sukkur Barrage is the oldest Barrage on the Indus River.

      The barrage was built during the British Raj from 1923 to 1932 

      It was named Lloyd Barrage.

      This barrage is located near the city of Sukkur in the Sindh province 


      Additional information

      Six barrages on the Indus River

      1. Guddu Barrage
      2. Sukkur Barrage
      3. Kotri Barrage 
      4. Taunsa Barrage
      5. Chashma Barrage
      6. Jinnah Barrage.

      The Indus River starts in Tibet

      It originates from the Mansarovar lake

      They flows through India, Pakistan,

      They finally fall into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

      It starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

      The old name of the Indus River was: Sindhu

      National River of Pakistan: Indus River

      Indus River Length: 3,180 km

      Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

      The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 

      The upper Indus Plain starts from Kalabagh

      ND11-2-2023

      318

      The 18th Constitutional Amendment Act was passed on _____?

      Correct Answer: 19th April 2010
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The 18th Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan was passed by the National Assembly on April 8, 2010, and by the Senate on April 15, 2010.

      It became law after President Asif Ali Zardari signed it on April 19, 2010.

      This amendment revoked the President's authority to dissolve Parliament.

      It resulted in 23 out of 26 proposed amendments being enacted, while three did not succeed.

      319

      Who is a Diana Baig?

      Correct Answer: Cricket player
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Diana Baig is a Pakistani women's cricket team fast bowler.
    • She has also played football for Pakistan at the international level.
      • 320

      2018 Election was the _____ General Elections in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 11th
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      2018 Election was the 11th General Elections in Pakistan held on 25 July 2018.

      Election in Pakistan
      Term NoElection NameWining Party
      11st elections : 1954Muslim League (Pakistan) - indirect elections
      22nd elections : 1962 non-party based indirect elections
      General Elections
      Term NoElection NameWining Party
      1General Election 1970Awami League (First General Election in histoty of Pakistan)
      2General Election 1977PPP
      3General Election 1985PML (non-party based elections)
      4General Election 1988PPP
      5General Election 1990Islami Jamhoori Ittihad
      6General Election 1993PPP
      7General Election 1997PMLN
      8General Election 2002PMLQ
      9General Election 2008PPP
      10General Election 2013PMLN
      11General Election 2018PTI


      321

      What was the voter turnout in General Elections 2018?

      Correct Answer: 51%
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Turnout in Pakistani general elections

      1970: 63%

      1988: 43%

      1990: 45%

      1993: 40%

      1997:35%

      2002: 41%

      2008: 44%

      2013: 53%

      2018: 51%

      322

      The sign ''see in detail'' in the picture means ______?

      Correct Answer: Men at work.
      Show explanation
      Explanation

    • The "Men at Work" sign warns drivers about road construction or maintenance ahead.
    • Drivers should slow down and proceed with caution to avoid hazards.
      • 323

      The sign 'see in detail' in the picture means?

      Correct Answer: Hump bridge.
      Show explanation
      Explanation

    • A hump bridge sign warns drivers about a raised bridge that may cause sudden elevation changes.
    • Drivers should slow down to avoid losing control, especially at high speeds.
      • 324

      The sign "see in detail" in the picture means?

      Correct Answer: Mind side wind.
      Show explanation
      Explanation


    • The "Mind side wind" sign warns drivers about strong crosswinds that may affect vehicle control.
    • It is commonly placed in open areas, bridges, or coastal roads where wind gusts are strong.
      • 325

      The sign (see in detail) in the picture means?

      Correct Answer: Level crossing without gate.
      Show explanation
      Explanation


    • A level crossing without a gate sign warns drivers of a railway crossing ahead that does not have barriers or gates.
    • Drivers should slow down, look both ways, and proceed with caution.
      • 326

      The sign "see in detail" in the picture means ______?

      Correct Answer: Narrow bridge.
      Show explanation
      Explanation

    • A narrow bridge sign typically shows two converging lines, indicating that the bridge ahead is narrower than the road.
    • Drivers should slow down and be cautious, especially if two-way traffic is present.
      • 327

      The sign 'see in detail' in the picture means _____?

      Correct Answer: 40 KM Speed restriction is lifted
      Show explanation
      Explanation


    • A speed limit restriction lifted sign usually has a black diagonal line crossing the speed number (e.g., 40).
    • This means the previous 40 KM speed limit no longer applies, and normal speed regulations resume.
      • 328

      The sign (see in detail) in the picture means _____?

      Correct Answer: Height is limited to 3.5 m.
      Show explanation
      Explanation


    • A height restriction sign typically shows a number with "m" (meters) inside a red circle, indicating the maximum allowed vehicle height.
    • It prevents tall vehicles from entering areas with low clearances, such as tunnels or bridges.
      • 329

      How can use the engine of your vehicle to control your speed?

      Correct Answer: By changing to a lower gear
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Downshifting to a lower gear increases engine braking, helping control speed without excessive use of brakes.
    • This technique is useful on steep descents to prevent brake overheating.
      • 330

      Right of way in Round about is for _____?

      Correct Answer: Both A and B
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Vehicles already in the roundabout have the right of way.
    • In most countries following right-hand traffic, vehicles coming from the right also have priority.
    • This rule ensures smooth and safe traffic flow in roundabouts.
      • 331

      Who cannot use the Motorway?

      Correct Answer: All of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Animal-driven carts, cyclists, and pedestrians are not allowed on motorways due to safety regulations.
    • Motorways are designed for fast-moving vehicles, and slow or non-motorized traffic can cause accidents.
      • 332

      On 24th October 2020, FATF decided to keep Pakistan Grey List till _____?

      Correct Answer: February 2021
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) reviewed Pakistan’s progress on October 24, 2020, and decided to keep it on the Grey List until February 2021.
    • Pakistan was required to complete its action plan to avoid further consequences.
      • 333

      The severity of 8th October 2005 earthquake in Pakistan on Richter scale was?

      Correct Answer: 7.6
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On the 8th October , 2005 most of the parts of Pakistan were jolted by a strong Earthquake, measuring 7.5 in the Richter scale which is believed to be the most powerful to hit this region in the past 100 years.
      334

      Who became the Prime Minister of Pakistan after the 2002 elections?

      Correct Answer: Zafarullah Khan Jamali
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Zafarullah Khan Jamali became Prime Minister on November 21, 2002, after the general elections.
      • He remained in office until June 26, 2004, when he resigned.
      • Pervez Musharraf (served as President from 2001 to 2008).
      335

      In which year Gen. Yahya held General Elections in the country?

      Correct Answer: 1970
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Agha Muhammad Yahya Kha was a Pakistani military officer and politician

      who served as the third president of Pakistan from 1969 to 1971.

      born: 4 February 1917 -  died: 10 August 1980

      By 28 July 1969, President Yahya Khan had set a framework for elections that were to be held in December 1970.

      Finally, the general elections were held all over the country.

      He also served as the commander-in-chief of the Pakistan Army from 1966 to 1971.

      ND14-7-2023

      Which pass connects Gilgit-baltistan with China?

      Correct Answer: Khunjerab Pass
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Khanjerab Pass is located in Gilgit–Baltistan's Hunza and Nagar Districts .

      Other name: Friendship Gate

      Connects: China with Pakistan 

      Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 

      Khunjerab Pass is a border connecting to China in the North of Pakistan

      Khunjerab Pass is located in the Karakoram range.

      338

      Chashma is situated in ______?

      Correct Answer: Kundian
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Chashma is located near Kundian, in Mianwali District, Punjab, Pakistan.
    • It is known for the Chashma Barrage and Chashma Nuclear Power Plant.
      • 339

      What was the Tenure of President Muhammad Ayoub Khan?

      Correct Answer: 27th October 1958 to 25th March 1969
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ayub Khan – Key Facts

      • Full Name: Muhammad Ayub Khan
      • Born: 14 May 1907, Rehana (Abbottabad District)
      • Died: April 1974, Islamabad
      • Education: Aligarh Muslim University (AMU)
      • Military Role: Army General, seized power from Iskander Mirza in 1958
      • Presidency: 27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969
      • Resigned: 25 March 1969
      • Constitution Enforced: 8 June 1962
      • Election Symbol: Red Rose

      Presidents of Pakistan:

      1. Iskander Mirza (23 March 1956 – 27 October 1958)
      2. Ayub Khan (27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969)
      3. Yahya Khan (25 March 1969 – 20 December 1971)
      4. Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (20 December 1971 – 14 August 1973)
      5. Fazal Ilahi Chaudhry (14 August 1973 – 16 September 1978)
      6. Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq (16 September 1978 – 17 August 1988)
      7. Ghulam Ishaq Khan (17 August 1988 – 18 July 1993)
      8. Farooq Leghari (14 November 1993 – 2 December 1997)
      9. Muhammad Rafiq Tarar (1 January 1998 – 20 June 2001)
      10. Pervez Musharraf (20 June 2001 – 18 August 2008)
      11. Asif Ali Zardari (9 September 2008 – 9 September 2013)
      12. Mamnoon Hussain (9 September 2013 – 9 September 2018)
      13. Arif Alvi (9 September 2018 – 10 March 2024)
      14. Asif Ali Zardari (10 March 2024 – Present)
      340

      In which year Islamia College for Women, Cooper Road, Lahore was established?

      Correct Answer: 1939
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Government Islamia College for Women, Cooper Road, Lahore was established in 1939

      It was established by Anjaman-e-Hamayat Islam. 

      It was the only higher education institution for women that the Father of the Nation, Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah, visited in 1946.


      Government Islamia College Civil Lines, formerly called Dayanand Anglo Vedic College

      Founded by Arya Samaj as the school of Dayanand Anglo Vedic on June 1, 1886,

      341

      Bicameral system of legislature was introduced in Pakistan under the _______ Constitution.

      Correct Answer: 1973
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Under the 1973 Constitution, Pakistan adopted a bicameral system at the centre, called “The Parliament”, composing the President, the National Assembly, and the Senate.


      342

      Monsoon season in Pakistan starts in July and ends in ____?

      Correct Answer: September
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Monsoon season in Pakistan starts in July and ends in September.
      • It bringing significant rainfall mainly to northern and eastern regions. 

      Additional information:

      • Cool, dry winter: December to February
      • Hot, dry spring: March to May
      • Summer rainy season (southwest monsoon): June to September
      • Retreating monsoons: October to November
      343

      Pakistan's Demographic and Health Survey is conducted by ______?

      Correct Answer: National Institute of Population Studies, Islamabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The National Institute of Population Studies (NIPS), Islamabad, conducts Pakistan's Demographic and Health Survey (PDHS).
    • The survey provides data on population, health, and nutrition indicators for policy-making.
      • 344

      Who was the first Minister for Population of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Dr. Attiya Inayatullah
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Dr. Attiya Inayatullah served as Pakistan's first female Minister for Population Welfare Affairs.
    • She played a significant role in shaping the country's population policies.
      • 345

      Hingol National Park, located in Makran District, Balochistan is famous for its ?

      Correct Answer: Mud Volcanoes
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hingol National Park is famous for its active mud volcanoes,


      It is the largest national park in Pakistan Located in the Makran coastal region.


      Area of about 6,100 square kilometres (2,400 sq mi).


      It is located 190 km from Karachi in the three districts of Gwadar, Lasbela and Awaran in Balochistan.


      Hingol was declared a national park in 1988.

      346

      Which is the Largest Cricket Stadium in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: National Stadium Karachi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The National Stadium Karachi has a seating capacity of approximately 34,000.

      This making it the largest cricket stadium in Pakistan.

      Qaddafi Stadium Lahore has a capacity of around 27,000.

      Iqbal Stadium Faisalabad has a capacity of about 18,000.

      347

      What is the name of economist who developed the current Human Development Index (HDI)?

      Correct Answer: Dr. Mahbub ul Haq
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistani economist Mahbub ul Haq created HDI in 1990.

      Haq was a politician and international development theorist.

      He served as Pakistan's Minister of Finance.

      The HDI is a widely used tool for measuring a country's development.

      348

      Who was the Viceroy of India at the time of the Quit India Movement?

      Correct Answer: Lord Linlithgow
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lord Linlithgow was the viceroy of India at the time of Quit India Movement.

      Started: Aug 8, 1942

      Started from: Bombay 

      Launched by: Gandhi

      Also known as: August Movement

      Demand: To end British rule

      Jinnah changed the slogan "Divide and Quit India".

      Lord Linlithgow was Viceroy of India from 1936 to 1944

      Started by party: Congress

      ****

      349

      Pakistan signed a frontier agreement with Iran in:

      Correct Answer: 1958
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In 1958, Iran and Pakistan signed an agreement in Tehran to clarify their border

      History

      They were both members of the Central Treaty Organization (CENTO) during the Cold War.

      Iran helped Pakistan in the 1965 and 1971 Indo-Pakistani Wars. 

      In 2004, Pakistan and Iran signed a Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA).

      The agreement became operational in 2006. 

      350

      The primary import commodity for Pakistan from Africa is

      Correct Answer: Copper
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan imports a wide range of commodities from Africa, including petroleum, coal, tea, cotton, and copper. 

      In 2020, Pakistan imported $2.25 billion worth of products from Africa. 

      In 2020, Pakistan imported $547 million worth of coffee and tea from Africa. 

      Pakistan also imports crude oil, iron, steel, inorganic chemicals, and textile products from Africa

      351

      The gateway to Central Asia is ?

      Correct Answer: Gwadar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       Gwadar Port of Pakistan is a gateway and economic route to the landlocked Central Asian Republics (CARs)

       Additional information 

      The Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in the Balochistan province of Pakistan

      The Gwadar Port was Opened: in 2002;

      Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from Oman on 8th September 1958.

      Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

      Gwadar remained under Omani rule for 174 years from 1783 -1958 

      Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in Balochistan province.

      Opened: 2002 

      352

      Which Indian Prime Minister visited Minar-e-Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Atal Bihari Vajpayee
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Indian Prime Minister who visited Minar-e-Pakistan is Atal Bihari Vajpayee.

      He was the first Indian leader to visit Minar-e-Pakistan on February 21, 1999.

      353

      In accordance with 25th Constitutional Amendment, the total senate seats will be reduced to ____ from existing 104 after the retirement of FATA Senators in 2024.

      Correct Answer: 96
      Show explanation

      In Senate: May 25th, 2018

      In Provincial Assembly of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: May 27th, 2018

      Assented to May 31st, 2018

      354

      What is the total length of Pakistan's International Borders?

      Correct Answer: 6,774
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The total length of Pakistan's international borders is 6,774 kilometers.

      Breakdown:

      • Pakistan-Afghanistan Border: 2,670 km

      • Pakistan-China Border: 559 km

      • Pakistan-India Border: 3,323 km

      • Pakistan-Iran Border: 959 km

      355

      Which city was called "The Switzerland of East" by queen Elizabeth II?

      Correct Answer: Sawat
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Swat District is a district in the Malakand Division of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

      Swat is the 15th-largest district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

      356

      Which document is considered the precursor of the Two Nation Theory?

      Correct Answer: Lucknow Pact
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The Lucknow Pact (1916) was an agreement between the Indian National Congress and the All India Muslim League, recognizing the separate political identity of Muslims.
    • It laid the foundation for the Two Nation Theory, which later led to the demand for Pakistan.
      • 357

      Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established the British Indian Association in ______?

      Correct Answer: 1866
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sir Syed Ahmad Khan established the British Indian Association in Aligarh in 1866.

      The association's purpose was to convey the views of the Indian people to the British rulers. 

      358

      Wah Ordinance Factory was constructed with help of ______?

      Correct Answer: China
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Pakistan Ordnance Factories (POF) is a major firearm and defense contractor

      headquartered in Wah Cantt, Punjab, Pakistan.

      The POF was founded by the Government of Pakistan with the help of China.

      Founded: 1951

      Owner: Pakistan Ministry of Defence


      359

      Which river does not flow on Pakistan land?

      Correct Answer: Beas
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Beas River flows through India.
    • It does not enter Pakistan.
    • Ravi, Sutlej, and Gomal flow through Pakistan.
      • 360

      What is the point called where the Karakoram, Hindukush, and Himalayan mountain ranges meet?

      Correct Answer: Pamir Knot
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The point where the Karakoram, Hindu Kush, and Himalayan mountain ranges meet the Pamir Mountains

      It is near the junction of the borders of China, Pakistan, and Afghanistan. 

      The Tibetan Plateau (Pamir Plateau) is known as the Roof of the World.

      It's also the thickest crust in the world, between 70 and 90 kilometers.

      The plateau is the world's largest and highest.

      Its average elevation is more than 4,500 meters (14,764 feet).

      361

      The Mangla dam is a multipurpose dam on the river Jhelum in the _______?

      Correct Answer: Mirpur District, AJ&K
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Mangla Dam is a multipurpose dam on the Jhelum River in Pakistan’s Azad Jammu and Kashmir’s Mirpur District.

      It is the world’s sixth-largest dam.

      Mangla Dam is the first dam in Pakistan.

      In 1962 Mangla Dam was started.


      362

      When the constitution of 1962 was abrogated?

      Correct Answer: 25th March 1969
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      President Ayub Khan promulgated the Constitution of 1962 on 1 March 1962

      It finally came into effect on 8 June 1962

      In 1962 constitution:

      • The presidential government system was introduced 
      • Urdu and Bengali was Declared as the Official Language 

      Iskandar Ali Mirza, the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 


      Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation


      Ayoub Khan enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962


      Yahiya Khan abrogated the 1962 constitution and became CMLA

      363

      What is connected to Baluchistan through the Pamir-Chitral pass?

      Correct Answer: Afghanistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Pamir-Chitral pass is a high mountain pass that connects Chitral in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan to Afghanistan's Wakhan Corridor, which in turn connects to Baluchistan.

      364

      Pakistan became member of United Nation in which year?

      Correct Answer: 30 Sep 1947
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Pakistan became the 57th member of UNO on 30 Sep 1947.
      Additional information
      Afghanistan opposed Pakistan’s membership in the United Nations due to the Durand Line issue.
      The United Nations is headquartered in the Midtown Manhattan neighborhood of New York City, New York, United States.
      UNO Founded: October 24, 1945,
      San Francisco, California, United States
      UNO Membership:
      193 member states; 2 observer states
      UNO Official languages:
      (06) Arabic;
      Chinese;
      English;
      French;
      Russian;
      Spanish;

      365

      The first Nuclear Power Plant (KANUPP) was set up in Pakistan in _____?

      Correct Answer: 1972
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The first nuclear power plant in Pakistan was established in Karachi With the help of Canada 

      KANUPP is Pakistan's first nuclear power plant, inaugurated on November 28, 1972, with a total gross capacity of 137 MW.

      while

      K2 & K3 with the help of China 

      K2  started on 21 May 2021 and has an 1100 MW capacity.

      K3 is expected to be started in 2022.

      366

      The first TV station was established in Pakistan in _____?

      Correct Answer: 1964
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      PTV was founded on 26 November 1964.

      It is the first official television station.

      PTV started its color transmission in 1976


      Additional information

      PTV started its live transmission on November 26, 1964
      On this date, it began its first transmission broadcasts from Lahore, followed by Dhaka.
      Ayub Khan inaugurated this first official transmission broadcasts 
      A third center was established in Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965 

      The fourth center was established in Karachi in 1966.

      Aslam Azhar is widely considered to be the "father of Pakistan Television".

      367

      In which Constitution of Pakistan, the Ministers were neither members of the parliament nor answerable to the Parliament?

      Correct Answer: 1962 constitution
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • The 1962 Constitution of Pakistan introduced a presidential system, where ministers were appointed by the president and were not members of the parliament.
    • They were directly accountable to the president, not the legislature.
    • The Constitution of 1962 was promulgated by President Ayub Khan on 1 March 1962 and finally came into effect on 8 June 1962.
      • 368

      Which Radio Station already existed at the time of creation of the Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: All of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan independence was announced first through Lahore Radio station.

      Pakistan’s first radio station was setup in Karachi.

       

      There are the following three radio stations existed at the time of independence of Pakistan on 14 August 1947.

      1.      Peshawar Radio Station established in 1935

      2.      Lahore radio station established in 1937

      3.      Dhaka radio station established in 1939

      ******


      369

      Parachinar is the main town of ______?

      Correct Answer: Khyber Agency
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Parachinar is a town and the capital of the Kurram District in the province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

      It is mainly inhabited by Pashtun.

      Parachinar is situated on the west of Peshawar

      Parachinar is the closest point in Pakistan to Kabul.

      370

      Which one is the Largest Canal in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Nara Canal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Nara Canal is the longest canal in the country.

      It is approximately 364 kilometers (226 miles) long.

      It plays a vital role in irrigating a vast area of land in Sindh province.

      The canal's water source is the Indus River,

      371

      The Longest Canal in Pakistan is _____?

      Correct Answer: Nara Canal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      ·         The Nara Canal is a deepened delta channel of the Indus River in Sindh province.

      ·         The canal runs from above the Sukkur Barrage through the districts of Khairpur, Sanghar, Mirpurkhas and Tharparkar to the Jamrao Canal.

      ·         The Nara is the longest canal in Pakistan, running for about 226 mi (364 km).

      372

      Who was the first British ambassador to the Mughal's India court?

      Correct Answer: Sir Thomas Roe
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • Sir Thomas Roe was the first official British ambassador to the Mughal court.
    • He was sent by King James I in 1615.
    • He visited Emperor Jahangir's court to secure trade privileges for the East India Company.
      • 373

      The real name of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar was ______?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman Marvandi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The full name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar: Syed Shah Hussain Marwandi

      Lal Shahbaz Qalandar wrote several books in Persian and Arabic

      Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan

      Born: 1177, Marand, Iran

      Died: February 19, 1274, Sehwān

      Place of burial: Darbar Hazrat Usman Marvandi Lal Shahbaz Qalandar, Sehwān

      Title: Qalandar

      The shrine of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar is situated in Sehwan Sindh

      ND18-5-2023
      ND17-7-2023



      374

      Sindhi language is based on _____ script.

      Correct Answer: Arabic
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sindhi is descended from Old Indo-Aryan (Sanskrit)

      ﻿Sindhi is a branch of Indo-Iranian like Hindi, Punjabi, and other major languages in South Asia,

      Sindhi is written in a version of the Arabic script.

      Sindhi is the official language of the Pakistani province of Sindh.

      The Sindhi language is the oldest language

      Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam (the gateway of Islam), as it saw the first spread of Islam into South Asia.

      Sindh is the third-largest province of Pakistan by land area

      Sindh is the second-largest province by population after Punjab

      Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 district

      There are three Barrages in Sindh

      The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range.

      The Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2019 won by ______?

      Correct Answer: Quetta Gladiators
      Show explanation

      Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2019

      Edition: Fourth

      Dates: 14 Feb – 17 Mar 2019

      Administrator: PCB

      Format: Twenty20

      Venue: UAE & Pakistan

      Final venue: Karachi

      Winner: Quetta Gladiators (1st title)

      Quetta Gladiators won by: 8 wickets

      Runner-up: Peshawar Zalmi

      Participants: 6

      Matches: 34

      Player of the series: Shane Watson (Quetta Gladiators)

      Shane Watson made: 430 runs

      Most runs: Shane Watson

      Most wickets: Hasan Ali (Peshawar Zalmi)

      Hasan Ali took: 25 wickets

      "Game Changer" is the autobiography of which famous cricketer?

    • The book covers Afridi’s cricket career, controversies, and views on Pakistan cricket and politics.

      Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 district

      Sindhi is the official language of the Pakistani province of Sindh.

      Correct Answer: Krishna Kumari
      Show explanation

    • On Women’s Day, 2019, Krishna Kumari Kohli became the first-ever Hindu Dalit woman to chair a session of the Pakistan Senate.
    • She is a Pakistan People's Party (PPP) senator from Sindh.

      K-2 peak is the ____ highest peak of the World.

      Correct Answer: Second
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • K2 is part of the Karakoram Range.
        • It is located on the border between Baltistan, in the Gilgit–Baltistan region of Pakistan.
        • K-2 is also known as Godwin Austin.
        • 'K' standing for Karakoram.
        • Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.
        • Mount K2 is located on the border of Pakistan and China.
        • The height of K2 is 8,611 meters.

      • These elections were conducted for 16 seats in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly after FATA's merger with the province in 2018.
        • 383

        Fall of East Pakistan was on _____?

        Correct Answer: 16 December 1971
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        On 16 December 1971, East Pakistan was liberated from Pakistan as the newly independent state of Bangladesh.

        East Pakistan was the eastern provincial wing of Pakistan between 1947 and 1971.

        Its land borders were with India and Burma, with a coastline on the Bay of Bengal.

        India firstly recognized to Bangladesh

        The Central National Muhammadan Association in 1877 was founded by _____?
