        386

        Which Foreign Airline started it's service for Pakistan on 11th December 2020?

        Correct Answer: Virgin Atlantic
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Virgin Atlantic commenced its services to Pakistan on 11th December 2020.

        It is operating flights from London Heathrow and Manchester to Islamabad and Lahore.

        387

        Bari Doab lies between which two rivers?

        Correct Answer: Bias & Ravi
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Five Important Doabs of Pakistan 

        Sindh Sagar Doab is b/w: Indus & Jhelum rivers 

        Jech Doab is b/w: Jhelum & Chenab rivers 

        Bari Doab is b/w: Ravi & Sutlej rivers

        Rachna Doab  is b/w: Ravi  & Chenab rivers  

        Bias Doab is b/w: Bias & Sutlej rivers 

        Doab Chaj is b/w: Chenab and Jhelum.



        388

        The third Khilafat Conference was held in 1921 in which city?

        Correct Answer: Amritsar
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        1. First Khilafat Conference: Held in Bombay (now Mumbai) in July 1919.
        2. Second Khilafat Conference: Held in Delhi in November 1919.
        3. Third Khilafat Conference: Held in Amritsar in December 1919.
        4. Fourth Khilafat Conference: Held in Allahabad in July 1920.
        5. Fifth Khilafat Conference: Held in Calcutta (now Kolkata) in November 1920.
        6. Sixth Khilafat Conference: Held in Nagpur in December 1920.
        7. Seventh Khilafat Conference: Held in Karachi in February 1921.
        8. Eighth Khilafat Conference: Held in Delhi in November 1921.
        389

        The capital of British India was transferred from Calcutta to Delhi in which year?

        Correct Answer: 12th December 1911
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The capital of British India was transferred from Calcutta (now Kolkata) to Delhi in the year 1911.

        The announcement to shift the capital was made during the Delhi Durbar  held in December 1911 to commemorate the coronation of King George V.


        The new capital in Delhi was known as New Delhi and was designed by the architect Edwin Lutyens.

        It became the center of British colonial administration in India.

        390

        The communal award declared in 1932 by Ramsay McDonald provided a separate electorate for the ______?

        Correct Answer: Muslims
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • The Communal Award of 1932, declared by Ramsay McDonald, granted a separate electorate for Muslims in India.
      • It was part of efforts to address demands for greater political representation for different communities.
        • 391

        Which of the following movements truly reflected the spirit of Hindu-Muslim Unity?

        Correct Answer: Khilafat movement
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • The Khilafat Movement (1919-1924) aimed to protect the Ottoman Caliphate and brought together Hindus and Muslims in a common cause.
      • It played a key role in fostering Hindu-Muslim unity during the Indian independence struggle.
        • 392

        Which one of the following was the first fort constructed by the British in India?

        Correct Answer: Fort St.George
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Fort St. George, the first English fortress in India.
      • It was founded in 1639 in Madras (now Chennai).
      • Built by the British East India Company in 1640, it became a citadel by 1644.
      • It houses St. Mary's Church, a museum with British Rule artifacts, and Wellesley Residence with paintings of the fortress Governor.
        • 393

        Where is Pakistan Chowk? Why it is called Pakistani chowk?

        Correct Answer: See the detial about Pakistan Chowk
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan Chowk in Dera Ghazi Khan is a commercial area that connects all four provinces of Pakistan. 

        Significance of Pakistan Chowk:

        • It holds importance in the context of Dera Ghazi Khan (DG Khan).
        • It is a central location in Pakistan
        • 102 km west of Balochistan.
        • 229 km north to Sindh.
        • 217 km south of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
        • Eastward, it connects to Punjab.

        A.O Hume was associated with _____?

        Correct Answer: Civil services

      • He is best known as the founder of the Indian National Congress (INC) in 1885.

        396

        What was the chief characteristic of Government of India Act, 1935?

        Correct Answer: Federal Government at Centre
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • The Government of India Act, 1935 introduced a federal structure, dividing powers between the Centre and Provinces.
      • However, the federal part was never fully implemented, as princely states did not join the federation.
        • 397

        In violation of Indus Basin Treaty 1960, India has constructed Wullar Barrage on River _______?

        Correct Answer: Jhelum
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Indus Basin Treaty constructed the Wullar Barrage on the Jhelum River:

        Indus Basin Treaty History:

        The treaty was signed on 19th September 1960

        The Treaty was signed at Karachi:

        • by Field Marshal Mohammad Ayub Khan

        • the then President of Pakistan, Shri Jawaharlal Nehru

        • the then-Indian Prime Minister

        • and Mr. W.A.B. Illif of the World Bank

        The Treaty is effective from 1st April 1960 

        398

        The fundamental Rights, according to the law in Pakistan, are enforceable through:

        Correct Answer: Both A and B
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In Pakistan, fundamental rights are enforceable through the Supreme Court and High Courts

        The Supreme Court can enforce fundamental rights by entertaining cases under Article 184(3) of the Constitution

        Correct Answer: Shanghai Cooperation (SCO)
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Pakistan, China, and Russia are key members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO), a political, economic, and security alliance.
      • Pakistan became a full member in 2017, strengthening its ties with regional powers.

        Which international treaty has Pakistan refused to sign, citing concerns about national security?

        Correct Answer: Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT)
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Pakistan has not signed the NPT due to concerns over its national security and nuclear deterrence against regional threats.
      • India also refused to sign the NPT, as both countries consider it discriminatory.

        Pakistan faced severe flooding in 2022-2023, prompting international aid and collaboration. Which organization led a $1.5 billion aid package for Pakistan’s flood relief efforts?

        Correct Answer: World Bank
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The organization that led a $1.5 billion aid package for Pakistan’s flood relief efforts in 2022-2023 is the World Bank.

        The World Bank played a significant role in providing financial assistance to Pakistan to address the devastating impacts of the floods.

        The aid package was aimed at supporting recovery, reconstruction, and resilience-building efforts in the affected areas.

        405

        Who was the first Chief Justice of Pakistan, and what significant decision is he known for in Pakistan’s early judicial history?

        Correct Answer: Justice Abdur Rashid, who presided over the oath of the first Governor-General
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Justice Abdur Rashid was the first Chief Justice of Pakistan (1949-1954).

        He is known for administering the oath to Muhammad Ali Jinnah as the first Governor-General of Pakistan.

        406

        Non-Accession clause was mentioned in ______?

        Correct Answer: Cripps Mission
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • The Cripps Mission (1942) proposed dominion status for India after WWII and included a Non-Accession clause, allowing provinces to opt out.
      • This clause was significant as it suggested that princely states and provinces could choose not to join the Indian Union.
        • 407

        The book entitled "Muhammad Ali Jinnah" was written by ______?

        Correct Answer: M.H Saiyid
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • "Muhammad Ali Jinnah" was written by M.H. Saiyid.
      • He highlighting Jinnah’s political career and role in the creation of Pakistan.
      • The book provides insights into Jinnah’s leadership, vision, and struggles for independence.

        The book "My Indian Years" was written by ______?

        Correct Answer: Lord Hardinge
        Show explanation
        Explanation

      • He served as Viceroy of India (1910–1916).
      • The book provides insights into British administration and policies in India during his tenure.

        Mohammad Bin Qasim was tortured to death in a prison in Iraq during the reign of _____?

        Correct Answer: Sulaiman
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Habib bin Muhallab was the governor of Sindh after the death of Muhammad Bin Qasim.

        Mohammad Bin Qasim, the Arab conqueror of Sindh, was tortured to death in 715 CE during the reign of Caliph Sulaiman.

        412

        During Aurangzeb's reign, which of the following were not included in the Government?

        Correct Answer: Marhattas
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        During the Aurangazeb's reign, Marathas were not included in his government.

        During his reign, the Mughal Empire extended from the Himalayas to Karnataka and Gujarat to Bengal.

        However he faced lot of rebels from the Jat, Maratha as well as Sikh community.

        413

        Who succeeded Siraj-ud-Daula as Nawab of Bengal after the Battle of Plassey?

        Correct Answer: Mir Jafar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        After the Battle of Plassey in 1757, Mir Jafar was installed as the Nawab of Bengal by the British East India Company.

        War of Plassey was fought on 23 June 1757.

        This war was fought between Lord Clive and Nawab Siraj-ud-Daula.

        414

        Which of the following was built by Akbar?

        Correct Answer: Agra Fort
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Agra Fort: Built by Akbar.

        Lahore Fort: Built by Akbar in 1566 AD, using burnt brick masonry.

        Fatehpur Sikri: A fortified city in Uttar Pradesh, India, built by Akbar.

        415

        The first amendment to the Constitution of 1973 which allowed every citizen to form a political party was passed in the year of _______?

        Correct Answer: 1973
        Show explanation
        Explanation

      • It allowed every citizen the right to form a political party.

        "The Last Days of United Pakistan" was written by _____?

        Correct Answer: G.W. Chaudhary
        Show explanation

      • The book discusses the political and historical events leading to the separation of East Pakistan in 1971.

        The only Indian on the Viceroy Lord Mountbatten's Staff was _______?

        Correct Answer: V.P. Menon
        Show explanation
        Explanation

      • He played a key role in negotiating princely states' accession to India.

        Jinnah Stadium is located in ________?

        Correct Answer: Gujranwala
        Show explanation

      • It is mainly used for cricket and football matches.
      • It has hosted international and domestic cricket games, including Pakistan’s home matches.
        • 419

        Roosevelt hotel of PIA is located in which country?

        Correct Answer: New York
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Roosevelt Hotel in New York City has been owned.

        It is operated by Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) since 1979.

        It is a historic hotel located in Midtown Manhattan.

        PIA has maintained ownership and operation of the hotel as a subsidiary asset.

        420

        PIC stands for ______?

        Correct Answer: Pakistan Information Commission
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Pakistan Information Commission (PIC) ensures the implementation of the Right of Access to Information Act, 2017.
      • It facilitates citizens' access to public records for transparency and accountability.
        • 421

        Which of the following country was host the 14th South Asian Games 2021?

        Correct Answer: Pakistan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan was set to host the 14th South Asian Games 2021, but the event was postponed.

        Pakistan will host the 14th South Asian Games (SAG) in 2025.

        The main host city will be Lahore, with some sports also being held in Faisalabad, Gujranwala, Islamabad, and Sialkot.

        422

        Pakistan hockey team won the Gold Medal for the second time in which Olympics?

        Correct Answer: 1968 Mexico City
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan has won a total of 04 gold medals in Olympic Games so far

        Pakistan won its first Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Hockey in Rome held in 1960.

        Pakistan won its second Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Hockey in Mexico held in 1968.

        Pakistan won its third Olympic Gold Medal in in the field of Hockey Loss Angels, USA held in 1984.

        Pakistan won its fourth Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Javelin Throw in Paris held in 2024.

         *****

        423

        Aligarh Institution Gazette was published in which language?

        Correct Answer: English and Urdu
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Aligarh Institute Gazette was published in 1866 by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.
      • It was later renamed in 1921 to Muslim University Gazette.
      • The Muslim University Gazette was eventually changed to Aligarh Muslim University Gazette.
        • 424

        Which of the following cities of Pakistan is famous due to stupas?

        Correct Answer: Swat
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        IN CTS EMT Rescue 1122 Solved Paper 26/1/2025 Both A and B Answers are correct 

         Taxila, Swat Valley, and Zarai  are cities in Pakistan that are famous for their stupas

        A stupa is a holy place for Buddhism

        It is also written as "stoopa."

        425

        Bhambore is an archaeological site located in _______?

        Correct Answer: Sindh
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Bhanbhore or Bhambhore, is a city dating to the 1st century BCE located in modern-day Sindh, Pakistan.

        The city ruins lie on the N-5 National Highway, east of Karachi.

        426

        Manora famous for tourism is the name of _____?

        Correct Answer: Island
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Manora is a group of associated islands of the Pakistani coastline along the Arabian Sea.

        It is also famous for Naval Base.

        It is located near Karachi.

        427

        NSP stands for ______?

        Correct Answer: National Support Program
        Show explanation

        • NSP stands for the National Support Program
        • A program aimed at curing severe acute malnutrition in Sindh, Pakistan.

        428

        Jamshoro is famous for its _____?

        Correct Answer: All of these
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Jamshoro is home to major educational institutions, including the University of Sindh and Mehran University.
      • It is also known for Sindhology research and heavy winds due to its geographical location.

        The credit of the present unanimous Constitution of Pakistan goes to ______?

        Correct Answer: Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Zulfikar Ali Bhutto is credited with the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan.

        The 1973 Constitution was unanimously adopted and remains Pakistan's current constitution.

        All India Muslim League 1st President: Sir Agha Khan

        All India Muslim League Founded: 30 December 1906 at Dacca, British India (now Dhaka, Bangladesh)

        All India Muslim League Headquarters: Lucknow

        All India Muslim League Dissolved: 14 August 1947

        431

        Hussain Shaheed Suharwardy was Pakistan’s ______?

        Correct Answer: Prime Minister
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hussain Shaheed Suharwardy was Pakistan’s Prime Minister.

        He served as the Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1956 to 1957.

        432

        In Pakistan, IRSA stands for ______?

        Correct Answer: Indus River System Authority
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Indus River System Authority (IRSA) is a water regulator in Pakistan,

        established in 1992 as an act of Parliament.

        Headquarters Islamabad, Pakistan

        Location

        Pakistan: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Gilgit-Baltistan, Punjab, Balochistan, Sindh & Azad Kashmir.

        433

        NRO Act issued by former President of Pakistan on _____?

        Correct Answer: 5 October 2007
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        NRO was an ordinance issued by the former President of Pakistan, General Pervez Musharraf, on 5 October 2007.

        NRO stands for National Reconciliation Ordinance.

        434

        The founder of Pakistan Atomic Program is?

        Correct Answer: Abdul Qadeer Khan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan is called father of Pakistan's atomic weapons program.

        Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan died on 10 October 2021, at the age of 85 after being transferred to a hospital in Islamabad with lung problems.


        435

        It restored the powers of the President to dissolve the National Assembly?

        Correct Answer: 17th amendment
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        The 17th Amendment, passed in 2003, restored the powers of the President to dissolve the National Assembly, a power that had been removed by the 13th Amendment in 1997.
        This amendment was part of a deal between the government and the MMA (Muttahida Majlis-e-Amal).
        It is also validated certain actions taken by General Pervez Musharraf after the 1999 coup.
        436

        Identify as to who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan only for one day?

        Correct Answer: I.I. Chundrigar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Prime Minister of Pakistan who served for only one day was I.I. Chundrigar.

        I.I. Chundrigar became the Prime Minister of Pakistan on October 18, 1957.

        But his government collapsed the very next day due to political instability and lack of support from coalition partners.

        437

        Which one of the following is called the only green desert of the world?

        Correct Answer: Thar Desert
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Thar Desert is often called the "only green desert of the world."
      • Unlike most deserts, it experiences a monsoon season, leading to lush vegetation.
        • 438

        SEPRA stands for _____?

        Correct Answer: Sindh Electric Power Regulatory Authority
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Sindh Electric Power Regulatory Authority (SEPRA) regulates electric power in Sindh.
      • It oversees policies and compliance in the energy sector of the province.
        • 439

        In which year PIA was established?

        Correct Answer: 1955
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In 1955, Orient Airways merged with a newly formed state-owned entity.

        This resulting in the establishment of Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) on March 11, 1955.

        PIA serves as Pakistan's national flag carrier.

        440

        The highest pass of Pakistan is _____?

        Correct Answer: Muztagh Pass
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • Muztagh Pass is the highest pass in Pakistan.
      • It is located in the Karakoram Range near the China border.

        Which one of the following was not a member of the Cabinet Mission Plan?

        Correct Answer: Lord Curnab
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Cabinet Mission was sent to India in February 1946.

        They arrived in India on March 24, 1946.

        The Cabinet Mission Plan was announced on May 16, 1946.

        The plan was created by the Cabinet Mission, which was sent to India by British Prime Minister Clement Attlee to discuss transferring power from the British to the Indians.

        The mission's members (3) were Lord Pethick-Lawrence, Sir Stafford Cripps, and A. V. Alexander.

        442

        Which country is considered as Corner Stone of Pakistan's Foreign Policy?

        Correct Answer: China
        Show explanation
        Explanation
      • China is the cornerstone of Pakistan's foreign policy due to strong diplomatic, economic, and defense ties.
      • CPEC is a key project enhancing their strategic partnership.
      • Pakistan and China share a deep friendship, with China calling Pakistan its Iron Brother.
      • Their bilateral relations are based on mutual trust, respect, and goodwill.

        Where is the National Museum of Pakistan located?

        Situated in Burns Garden on Dr. Zia-ud-Din Ahmed Road


        Established on 17 April 1950.


        There are approximately 16 museums in Pakistan.


        444

        Gashoo lake is located in _____?

        Correct Answer: Gilgit-Baltistan
        Show explanation

        445

        Who is set to become the second female judge of Supreme Court of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Musarrat Hilali

        ***
        17 judges are there in the Supreme Court of Pakistan.

        Retirement age of Supreme Court Judges: 65

        Retiring age of High Court Judge: 62

        *****

        Justice Hilali became only the second female judge to rise to the apex court after Justice Ayesha  Malik

        Justice Musarrat Hilali is set to become the first female chief justice of the Peshawar High Court (PHC) on April 1, 2023

        Syeda Tahira Safdaris a Pakistani jurist served as the Chief Justice of the Balochistan High Court.

        Mian Abdul Rasheed  was the 1st Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Pakistan

        447

        Kotri Barrage was constructed in which year?

        Correct Answer: 1955
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Kotri Barrage is also known as the Ghulam Muhammad Barrage.

        It is located near Hyderabad in Sindh, Pakistan.

        It was completed in 1955.

        It is situated on the Indus River between Jamshoro and Hyderabad.

        It is with four feeder canals—three on the left and one on the right bank—diverting water, including to Karachi.

        Who among the following was the first Tughlaq Sultan of Delhi?

        Correct Answer: Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq
        Show explanation

        who reigned over the Sultanate of Delhi from 1320 to 1325.

        He founded the city of Tughluqabad.

        449

        When Pakistan introduced National Identity cards (NIC)?

        Correct Answer: 1973
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan introduced National Identity cards (NIC) in 1973

        Pakistan initiated its National Identity Card System in 1973 under Article 30

        The card was first issued manually on hand-written paper in 1973

        the computerized card was issued in 2000

        IN 2012 the smart card was issued

        The first national identity card was issued by the authority to Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto.

      • Author: Imran Khan, co-authored by P. A. Khan
      • Published: 1993
      • Content: Chronicles the culture, traditions, and resilience of the tribal Pathans.
      • Focus: A personal and historical exploration of the Pathans' warrior spirit and way of life.

        Professional qualification of Quaid-e-Azam was _____?

        Correct Answer: Bar-at-Law
        Show explanation

        452

        Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar died during the ______ round table conference.

        Correct Answer: Second
        Show explanation
        Explanation

      • He attended the conference to represent the Indian Muslims but passed away due to illness.

      • He was died in London resting place Jerusalem (Bait-ul-Muqdis).
      • Comrade English newspaper 1911.

        Balochistan acquired the status of province in the which year?

        Correct Answer: 1970
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Balochistan got the status of a province: on 1 July 1970.

        Balochistan was given the status of a province during the era of Yahya Khan. 

        "Baluchistan" important question and information:

        Baluchistan covers 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

        Baluchistan province is located in South West of Pakistan.

          Baluchistan became a province in 1970.

            Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

              Baluchistan is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

              Balochistan is located on the Iranian plateau.

              Sajji dish belongs to Balochistan

              35 districts are there in Balochistan

              There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

              Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

              Baluchistan province has 7 divisions.

              454

              Balloki barrage is located in _____?

              Correct Answer: Ravi river
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Head Balloki or Balloki Headworks is a barrage on the Ravi River in the Punjab.

              It was first built around in 1915 in British India as part of the 'Triple Canals Project' to feed the Lower Bari Doab Canal. 

              455

              What is the meaning of Zarb-e-Azb?

              Correct Answer: Sharp & Cutting Strike
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              • Zarb-e Azab. 'Azb' refers to one of the seven swords of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) that he carried along in the Ghazwaz of Uhad and Badr to strike hard infidels.
              • Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad Launched on February 22, 2017,
              • the intensive nationwide operation against terror groups steered
              • by Army Chief General Qamar Javid Bajwa
              • Operation Zarb-e-Azb started by Army Staff General Raheel Sharif 
              • Operation Zarb-e-Azb start from 15 June 2014 till 22 February 2017
              • Location: Pakistan, North Waziristan Agency, Federally Administered Tribal Area, Karachi, Balochistan, Punjab
              • RK11-12-22
              456

              Karakoram Highway is also known as _____?

              Correct Answer: N-35
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Karakoram Highway (often abbreviated to KKH)

              The Karakoram Highway, also known as the Friendship Highway in China, was built by the governments of Pakistan and China

              Karakoram highway is also known as N-35

              The Karakoram Highway starts from Hassan Abdal.

              Karakoram highway was completed in 1979.

              its length in Pakistan is 1300 km.

              other related question 

              The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

              Karakoram Range is a mountain range located between China and Pakistan

              The road connecting Pakistan and China is called Karakoram Highway

              Khunjerab pass connects Pakistan with China

              ND19-3-2023

              457

              Where is Rakaposhi Glacier located in Gilgit-Baltistan?

              Correct Answer: Nagar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Rakaposhi 7788-M is a mountain in the Karakorum mountain range in Pakistan.

              It is situated in the Nagar Valley approximately 100 km north of the city of Gilgit.

              Rakaposhi is the only mountain in the world with more than 5,000 meters height between base camp and the summit



              458

              The most ancient city in South Asia is ______?

              Correct Answer: Peshawar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Peshawar, originally known as Purushapura.

              It is one of the oldest cities in South Asia.

              It dating back to at least 539 BCE.

              What document was firstly drafted to give pace to constitution making process?

              Correct Answer: Objective Resolution

              Explanation

              The Objectives Resolution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan on March 12, 1949.

              Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan, had presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949.

              Out of 75 members of the assembly, 21 voted for opposing it

              461

              Gaddani Beach is famous for ___?

              Correct Answer: Ship Breaking
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Gadani Ship-Breaking Yard is located on the Arabian Sea.

              It is 40 kilometers northwest of Karachi.

              It is located Gadani, District Lasbela , Balochistan.

              It's the third largest ship-breaking yard in the world.

              It is with 132 ship-breaking plots along a 10 kilometer stretch of beach.

              The yard is known as the "graveyard of ships". 
              462

              Naval War College is situated in ____?

              Correct Answer: Lahore
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The genesis of Pakistan Navy War College started with Pakistan Navy Staff School at Manora Island off Karachi in 1968.

              It was later upgraded to War College in 1971  and shifted to PNS Karsaz.

              In 1995, the College was shifted to Lahore with a dedicated building on Mall Road, which was inaugurated in August 1996.

              As of 2014, a new purpose-built campus had been constructed in Walton, where the war college was finally shifted to.

              297 general seats,
              66 reserved for women,
              8 reserved for non-Muslims.

              Who was the First Pakistani to climb K-2?

              Correct Answer: Ashraf Aman
              Show explanation
              Explanation

                • He became the first Pakistani to summit K2 in 1977.

                • Pakistani climber Muhammad Ali Sadpara went missing on K2 with two foreign climbers on 5 February 2021.
                • Samina Baig became the first Pakistani woman to climb K2 in July 2022.

                Baltistan is called ______?

                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Baltistan is a mountainous region in Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan-administered Kashmir.

                The average altitude of the region is over 3,350 meters (10,990 ft).

                Baltistan is largely administered under the Baltistan Division.

                ***
                470

                Name the first indigenous Electro-Optical satellite launched by Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: EO-1
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan's first indigenous EO-1 satellite launched into space on 17 January 2025.

                 

                Pakistan’s Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO)'s first fully indigenous Electro-Optical (EO-1) satellite was successfully lunched into space from China.

                 

                The satellite was launched from China’s Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre, with the event broadcast live from SUPARCO’s Karachi complex, allowing Pakistanis to watch the satellite’s journey into space.

                 ***

                The only government in India headed by the All-India Muslim League in 1946 was that of ______?

              • It was led by Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy as the Chief Minister.

                He was born on November 9, 1877, in Sialkot.

                476

                Jinnah Sikandar Pact was signed in October 1937 at ____?

                Correct Answer: Lucknow
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • Jinnah-Sikandar Pact was signed in October 1937 in Lucknow, British India.
              • The pact was between Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Sir Sikandar Hayat Khan.
              • It aimed to merge the Muslim elements of the Unionist Party with the All India Muslim League.
              • The goal was to create a united Muslim front to safeguard Muslim interests in Punjab and across India.

                Pakistan recognized China in ________?

                Correct Answer: 1950
                Show explanation

                Pakistan and China established diplomatic relations on 21 May 1951.

                Margalla Hills, a hill range, is a part of which mountain range?

                Correct Answer: Himalayas
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Margalla Hills are part of the Himalayan foothills. 

                The Margalla Hills is a hill range located north of Islamabad and south of Haripur District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                The Margalla Hills within the Margalla Hills National Park 

                The Margalla range has an area of 12,605 hectares.

                The highest peak of Margalla Hills is Tilla Charouni

                Elevation: 1,604 m (5,262 ft)

                480

                Rohtas Fort was built on the order of Sher Shah Suri by ______?

                Correct Answer: Raja Todar Mal
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Todar Mal, the minister of Sher Shah Suri, supervised the construction of Rohtas Fort.

                Rohtas Fort near Jehlum built by Sher Shah Suri 

                Sher Shah Suri was the founder of the Suri Empire in India, with its capital in Sasaram in modern-day Bihar. 

                Sher Shah Suri didn't live to see its completion as he died in 1545 before the fort was completed.

                Rohtas Fort is a 16th-century fortress

                Rohtas Fort is located near the city of

                Dina in Jhelum district of the Punjab province of Pakistan

                The construction of Rohtas Fort started in 1541 

                Rohtas Fort has 14 gates 

                481

                Which year did Pakistan play its first test match?

                Correct Answer: 1952
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • Pakistan played its first Test match in Delhi in October 1952, as part of a five-Test series.
              • India won the series 2–1.
              • Pakistan's first tour of England was in 1954, where the series ended in a 1–1 draw.
              • Pakistan achieved a memorable victory at The Oval, with Fazal Mahmood taking 12 wickets.
                • 482

                When Pakistan signed the Baghdad pact, later called CENTO?

                Correct Answer: 23 September 1955
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                • Pakistan joined the CENTO on 23 September 1955 and left in 1979
                • The Central Treaty Organization (CENTO),
                • Formerly known as the Middle East Treaty Organization (METO)
                • Also known as the Baghdad Pact
                • Military alliance of the Cold War.
                • Headquarters:
                  • Baghdad (1955 – 1958)
                  • Ankara (1958 – 1979)
                • Formed on 24 February 1955 by:
                  • Iran
                  • Iraq
                  • Pakistan
                  • Turkey
                  • and the United Kingdom.

                Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

                483

                Who was the first martial administrator who conducted general elections?

                Correct Answer: Yahya Khan
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • On 25th March 1969, the second martial law was imposed in Pakistan.
              • General Agha Muhammad Yahya Khan assumed the roles of President and Chief Martial Law Administrator (CMLA).
              • He issued the Legal Framework Order (LFO) to establish the framework for elections.
              • The first-ever general elections in Pakistan were held on 7th December 1970.

                What was election symbol of Fatima Jinnah in Presidential Election 1965?

                Correct Answer: Laltain
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                In Pakistan, the second Presidential Elections were held on 2 January 1965.

                Fatima Jinnah was a presidential candidate against Ayub Khan

                There were four candidates: Ayub Khan, Fatima Jinnah and two obscure people with no party affiliation. 

                Laltain was the election symbol of Fatima Jinnah in the Presidential Election in 1965


                Nominee
                		Ayub Khan
                		Fatima Jinnah
                Party

                Convention Muslim League

                (CML)


                Election symbol: Rose

                Combined Opposition Parties

                (COP)


                Election symbol: Laltain

                Electoral vote
                		49,951
                		28,691
                Percentage
                		62.43%
                		35.86%

                Question-related to Fatima Jinnah

                Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                Fatima Jinnah born: 30th July 1893

                Fatima Jinnah is referred as Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

                Fatima Jinnah died: on 9 July 1967

                Fatima Jinnah died at the age: of 71

                Fatima Jinnah died: Karachi

                Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon

                485

                Khawaja Nazimuddin belonged to ______?

                Correct Answer: East Pakistan (now Bangladesh)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Khawaja Nazimuddin was born on 19 July 1894.

                Khawaja Nazimuddin and Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy both were Pakistani Bengali barrister and politician.

                Khawaja Nazimuddin was the second prime minister of Pakistan from 1951 to 1953.

                Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy served as 5th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 12 September 1956 – 17 October 1957.

                486

                When was Makran acceded to Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: March 17, 1948
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistani Makran, a former princely state that acceded in 1948, was constituted after 1955 a district of Kalāt division. 

                Makran acceded to Pakistan on March 17, 1948.

                Makran was a princely state ruled by the Gichki Nawabs, who were of Rajput origin.

                The Gichki tribe settled in the Gichk valley of Panjgur and ruled the state of Makran from 1740 until 1955. 

                *****

                487

                Which country's architect designed the Islamabad map?

                Correct Answer: Greece
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • The master plan for Islamabad was designed by the Greek architect Constantinos Apostolou Doxiadis.
              • He was the lead architect for Islamabad, the new capital of Pakistan, in the 1960s.
              • His work focused on the science of human settlements and urban planning.
                • 488

                The Indo-Pak war on Rann of Kutch occurred in which year?

                Correct Answer: April 1965
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                On April 9, 1965, the Pakistani army attacked the Sardar and Tak posts in the Rann of Kutch. 

                The Pakistani army called the operation "Operation Desert Hawk 1".

                India and Pakistan signed a ceasefire agreement on June 30, 1965. 

                489

                The first Pakistani postage stamp was made in which city?

                Correct Answer: Karachi
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • The first postal stamp of Pakistan was issued on 9th July 1948.
              • It was designed by Abdul Rehman Chughtai.
              • The stamp was approved by Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
              • It was issued to commemorate Pakistan's first Independence Day.
                • 490

                Shaheed Benazir Bhutto became first woman Prime Minister of Pakistan on ____?

                Correct Answer: December 2, 1988
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Benazir Bhutto

                • Born: June 21, 1953, in Karachi.
                • Assassinated: December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi.
                • Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007).
                • Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP).
                • Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.
                • Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977).

                Political Career:

                • Sworn in as First Woman Prime Minister of Pakistan on December 2, 1988.
                • First term as Prime Minister: 1988 to 1990.
                • Second term as Prime Minister: 1993 to 1996.
                • Farooq Laghari dissolved her second government.
                • Served as the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                Education Details:

                • Nursery School: Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.
                • College: Radcliffe College
                • University: University of Oxford
                491

                Quaid-e-Azam retained the control of which ministry in his own hands?

                Correct Answer: Ministry of State and Frontier Regions
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Quaid-i-Azam has decided that the new Ministry will be called “The Ministry of States and Frontier Regions”.

                1st Governor-General of Pakistan 

                Quaid e Azam was born on:25 December 1876 in Karachi

                Died on: 11 September 1948

                In-office: 14 August 1947 – 11 September 1948

                Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan

                Succeeded by Khawaja Nazimuddin

                ND29-5-2023


                492

                Which team joined the PSL in its 3rd season?

                Correct Answer: Multan Sultans
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • Multan Sultans joined the Pakistan Super League (PSL) in its 3rd season in 2018.
              • It became the sixth team in the tournament.
                • 493

                Abdul Sattar Edhi was passed away on _____?

                Correct Answer: 8th July 2016
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Abdul Sattar Edhi NI LPP GPA was a Pakistani humanitarian, philanthropist

                founded the Edhi Foundation, which runs the world's largest volunteer ambulance network,

                Abdul Sattar Edhi started welfare services in 1951.

                Born: February 28, 1928, Bantwa, India

                Abdul Sattar Edhi died on 8 July 2016 at the age of 88 in Karachi.

                Bilquis Bano Edhi was: the wife of Abdul Sattar Edhi (married in 1966)

                Bilquis Bano Edhi passed away at the age of 74 on April 15th, 2022

                Awards:

                Lenin Peace Prize

                Ramon Magsaysay Award for Public Service

                ****

                The sixth death anniversary of Abdul Sattar Edhi was observed on 8 July 2022


                State Bank of Pakistan issued coins to commemorate Abdul Sattar Edhi: 50 rupee coin


                A huge statue of Abdul Sattar Edhi was installed at the famous Hockey Chowk in Balochistan (Quetta).

                ND13-5-2023
                494

                Which legendary Pakistani cricketer has been inducted into PCB Hall of Fame 2024?

                Correct Answer: All Of These
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The following four Pakistan's cricketing icons have been inducted into PCB Hall of Fame 2024

                1.     Inzamam-ul-Haq

                2.     Misbah-ul-Haq

                3.     Mushtaq Mohammad

                4.     Saeed Anwar

                Additional Information

                PCB stands for Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB).

                As of Jan 2025, PCB Hall of Fame has recognised 14 legendary cricketers, with these four now joining the prestigious list.

                 

                495

                Abida Parveen is a renowned singer of which genre?

                Correct Answer: Folk and Classical
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Abida Parveen is a Pakistani Sufi Muslim singer, composer, and musician.

                She has been dubbed as the 'Queen of Sufi music'.

                Received the President's Pride of Performance award in 1982 and Sitara-e-Imtiaz in 2005 

                She was born in Larkana, Sindh, Pakistan in 1954.

                She began performing in the 1970s a

                And became internationally prominent in the 1990s

                496

                Kirthar national park is in which province of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Sindh
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Elevation of kirthar mountain 2,260 m (7,410 ft) 

                The highest peak of the Kirthar mountains is Zardak Peak at 7,430 ft (2,260 m).

                Kirthar Range, located along the Balochistan and Sindh provincial border.

                The Hill Station of Sindh at Gorakh, in Kirthar Mountains Range, off Dadu.

                497

                Zarb-e-Kaleem is a philosophical poetry book by Allama Iqbal in which language?

                Correct Answer: Urdu
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Zarb-e-Kaleem is a philosophical poetry book of Allama Iqbal in Urdu.

                It was published in 1936, two years before his death.

                498

                Name the economic imitative programme launched by Prime Minister Shahbaz Sharif on 31 December 2024?

                Correct Answer: Uraan Pakistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif launched the ‘Uraan Pakistan, Homegrown National Economic Plan’ outlining the National Economic Transformation Plan for 2024-29.

                Launched on 31 December 2024.

                ***

                499

                Which Pakistani cricketer has been nominated for the ICC Men's ODI Cricketer of the Year 2024?

                Correct Answer: Babar Azam
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan’s cricket star Babar Azam has been nominated for the prestigious ICC Men’s T20I Cricketer of the Year award 2024.

                He has been recognised for his solid performances in 2024.

                In 24 T20I matches, the Pakistani batter scored 738 runs at an average of 33.54, including six half-centuries and nearly 100 boundaries.


                Additional information: 

                Virat Kohli has been awarded the title of ICC men's ODI Cricketer of the year 2023.


                500

                Summer Olympics concluded in August 2024. What position has Pakistan got in this competition?

                Correct Answer: 62nd
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan secured 62nd ranking in the Summer Olympic Games 2024.

                Pakistan won Single Gold Medal in Javelin Throw category.

                USA secured first position by winning a total of 126 medals. 

                Olympic Games 2024 held in Paris, France from 26 July to 11 Aug 2024.
